entry.title: From Revenue and Demand Planning to MRP Production Planning – A technical view
entry.content:
As a follow-up of the functional overview, this article on the topic is the next one in the serie, focusing on how revenue planning and volume/demand planning in SAP Analytics Cloud can integrate with Material Requirement Planning (MRP) process and production planning in SAP S/4HANA, from the technical perspective.

 

From Sales Revenue planning ... 

As explained in the functional article, SAP Analytics Cloud is a real platform capable of dealing with any type of planning processes. For instance, Sales Revenue (as part of a larger Financial Planning process) could be adjusted using drivers if needed, and be represented by different granularity levels (Products, Regions, Time and Version), as per below screenshot. And in this screenshot below, we simply adjust the overall Sales Revenue target for 2025 and Product New Ebike (ID: P_EBIKE).

Sales Revenue targets

Now that Financial Targets have been inputted for our New Ebike product, let's approach Sales Planning from a more supply chain perspective, by converting it into Sales Volume or Sales Quantity. There are various methods to achieve this, but let's consider a straightforward example where each product has a predetermined price for each region (that could also be simulated if necessary). The derivation logic involves calculating the sales volume using the formula Volume = Sales Revenue / Price.

Sales Volume generated from Sales Revenue targets

 It can also be visualized as as Heatmap chart with prior year comparison.

Sales Volume Heat map chart

 

... to Volume/Demand planning ... 

The above Sales Volume now serves as the starting point for the Demand propagation, based on the Manufacturing network. In this example, we have one manufacturing location per region, which is maintained as an attribute of the Region dimension, and we use it to generate the Production Volume per plant.

Production Volume per plant

This setup now acts as the foundation for transferring data to the Planned Independent Requirement (PIR) API, which can be triggered by clicking the "Transfer PIR" button above. To initiate this process, we first select our New EBike product, then click the button. Technically, this button passes the selected line to a Webservice (using JSON) developed on the Business Technology platform, which then maps its content to the fields of the PIR API.

Transfer to SAP S/4HANA as a Planned Independent Requirement

Next, we switch to SAP S/4HANA and open the Display PIRs App to verify that our Planned Independent Requirement has been successfully transferred.

Display PIRs App

We select the different fields.

Field selection based on SAP Analytics Cloud input.

The result displays the exact same values as in SAP Analytics Cloud, but now they are represented as a Planned Independent Requirement within SAP S/4HANA.

Display PIR table

Within SAP S/4HANA, we can now fully run the MRP process for our New eBike product and generate planned orders accordingly, using the Manage Material Coverage App.

Manage Material Coverage App

Result after MRP Run - Planned orders generated

Conclusion

In summary, using SAP's suites of application like SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP S/4HANA, and SAP Business Technology Platform services, helps businesses streamline their planning and production processes. By accurately forecasting revenue and demand, and then aligning these forecasts with production plans, companies can reduce delays, avoid having too much inventory, and meet customer needs more effectively.

As highlighted previously, this approach is especially beneficial for small to medium-sized businesses, while larger companies might also use SAP Integrated Business Planning for even better results. Overall, this integrated planning process ensures efficient operations and better decision-making, giving businesses a competitive advantage.

Components

  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP BTP Integration Suite
  • Planned Independent Requirement API
  • Planned Independent Requirement Help

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More)
entry.content:
This series of posts gives an overview of user experience innovations in the cloud from the last six months, covering our whole portfolio, in particular SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502. It also gives an overview of innovations in our UI technologies, making it ever easier to build and integrate UIs.

AI is revolutionizing user experience, giving users capabilities unheard of just a few years ago. SAP is harnessing the power of AI to help business users work more effectively: Joule is your copilot for seamless access to a variety of products a user needs, now covering 80% of the most-used transactions. Generative AI embedded in business applications gives users unprecedented insights and convenience. Beyond that, we continue to provide new applications with a great user experience, as well as UX-enhancing features.

How is AI, and especially generative AI, changing user experience for business users? Essentially via one or more of these patterns:

  • Summarizing business data in an easy to understand way – in text or graphical form.
  • Guiding and Recommending options or actions based on the current business context.
  • Creating proposals for texts or business data and entities such as assignments, performance goals with milestones etc., often based on natural language input.
  • Helping you write texts to people internally and externally, with our AI writing assistant.
  • Interacting via natural language with your application, e.g. search, filtering, analytical queries – as an alternative to established UI interactions.
  • Assisting you with all of the above, wherever you are, for all our cloud products – via Joule, your copilot for SAP systems.

Our overarching goal is to provide a great user experience everywhere. AI is a powerful means for achieving that, but of course you can also have a great user experience in applications without embedded AI.

A good user experience also needs to address teamwork, not just the experience of each individual – in other words, helping SAP users collaborate and work together.

I’ve collected examples from our latest releases to show how we are doing this – starting with our cross-product UX innovations in Joule and central entry points before looking at product UX innovations. Here is what the series will cover; as each part becomes available in the next couple of weeks I will update this list with links to each post:

  1. Central UX Services (this post)
    • Joule – latest UX innovations
    • Central entry points and services:
      • SAP Start / SAP Build Work Zone
      • SAP Task Center
      • SAP Mobile Start
  2. ERP: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori launchpad (AI, Joule and More).
  3. HR: SAP SuccessFactors (AI, Joule and More).
  4. Spend: SAP Ariba and SAP Fieldglass.
  5. Selling: SAP Customer Experience and SAP Industry Cloud – Retail.
  6. Platform: SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.
  7. UI Design and Technology – web and mobile.

Before we get started: if you haven’t already seen them, do have a look at the overview of public cloud UX innovations I posted in February and July 2024:

  • SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more.
  • SAP UX Latest 2024 Innovations in Public Cloud: Joule, GenAI and More.

This series of posts includes Joule and AI UX highlights from the above products, but for a more comprehensive overview of our recent AI innovations and SAP’s Business AI strategy, with many UX examples, have a look at:

  • SAP Business AI: Release Highlights Q4 2024
  • White Paper: SAP Business AI.

Joule User Experience Innovations

Joule is the AI-powered copilot for SAP systems, offering a powerful new user experience, using natural language to access our cloud products and providing a seamless flow for the user.

Now, Joule is generally available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502, as well as for our central entry points in the web SAP Start and SAP Build Work Zone (both standard and advanced editions). You can find examples in the respective product sections in this series; this section focusses on generic capabilities recently introduced or coming soon for Joule. If you want an overview of what’s new, or want to set up Joule in your landscape, have a look at the documentation:

  • What’s New for Joule.
  • Integrating Joule with SAP Solutions.

Joule already covers 80% of the most-used transactions in SAP systems and 1,200+ skills, tailored to business roles across SAP’s cloud portfolio such as hiring manager, billing specialist, procurement manager etc.

Joule Available on Mobile

Joule is also now generally available for mobile, running natively with SAP Mobile Start. Figure 1 shows examples of Joule running on a mobile phone.

Figure 1: Examples showing Joule integrated in SAP Mobile Start, showing from left to right: the start page, the informational pattern accessing SAP Help Portal, and the transactional pattern used to interact with SAP S/4HANA Cloud and then with SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Four images are shown: the start page shows a “How can I help you” text, followed by some suggested prompts as buttons; the informational pattern shows the prompt “How can I customize a sales order”. Below that, the sixteen-line written answer is shown, followed by a “Search results” selector and three top search results. The navigational pattern shows the prompt “Show sales orders with delivery status not completed”, below that, the reply from Joule “Here’s what I’ve found” is followed by a card containing three lines and an “Open” button. A second transactional example with the prompt “Show profile of Maria Alvares”, followed by the result and a button for navigating to her profile.

On Apple devices you can now use Siri and Siri Shortcuts to seamlessly interact with Joule - which works even better with Apple Intelligence. Simply say "Hey Siri, ask SAP. Show my sales orders with delivery status not completed" and Joule will provide you with the respective content within the app.

Note that as of today, not all your prompts and web apps are supported on the phone; however, we are continuously adding further Joule business skills and scenarios.

Find out more here:

  • Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update

Joule Analytical Pattern coming soon

In Q1/2025 we plan to provide a controlled release of the new analytical pattern for Joule, for those products listed above for which Joule is available. General availability currently planned for Q2/2025 (as usual for forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change). It is powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud.

This means that Joule will soon support four interaction patterns, as shown in Figure 2.

  1. Informational pattern, for querying written documents, such as SAP documentation in the SAP Help Portal, or customer policies etc.
  2. Navigational pattern, for finding and navigating to applications.
  3. Transactional pattern, for interacting directly with business applicatinos
  4. Planned New: Analytical pattern, for answering analytical queries directly in Joule.

Figure 2: Joule Interaction Patterns. ALT text: four interaction patterns are shown: informational, with the prompt “what are the key rules for a team outing” followed by an eight-line answer and a link to a source document; navigational, showing a button “Go to Org Chart” after approving a position; transactional, with the text “Below are the new position details set to be hired starting today”, followed by a card summarizing the key details, and a prompt by the user “Send for approval”; Analytical, showing the prompt “What is the hiring versus attrition trend for this job role in the past 12 months?” and the answer shown as a chart with one line for hired and one for percentage attrition, plus numbers showing 124 similar roles hired and 6 role attrition.

Find out more about Joule for Analytics in this blog post:

  • Analytical Insights in Joule (powered by SAP Analytics Cloud), Controlled release in Q1 2025.

Outlook

We are working on making Joule available for further cloud products, as well as making it even more useful via agentic-AI, where Joule coordinates multi-agent systems. An AI agent can autonomously plan, reason, access external tools, work with other agents, and iteratively reflect on progress until it achieves its objective.

Joule will help SAP’s cloud users work faster, get instant insights, and achieve better outcomes by simply asking questions or telling the system what to do. Not only in English, but in their own language. We plan to allow users to interact with Joule in a growing number of languages including German, Spanish, French, and Portuguese. To get an idea of what is planned and what it could look like, accessing multiple products, using English and Spanish (with subtitles), have a look at this:

  • Video: AI is the new UI (3:00).

For more details of what is planned, have a look at the road map for Joule in the SAP Road Map Explorer:

  • Joule Road Map.

Central entry points and services

SAP Start

The integration with Joule in SAP Start is now generally available. The purpose of SAP Start is to bring together relevant business information from SAP for each user in one central place, and also to provide the one place to go to start your SAP applications in the cloud. SAP Start:

  • Gives you quick access to all your applications via the simple yet powerful search field;
  • New: Gives you direct access to your product home pages, as shown in Figure 3.
  • Proactively informs you about your to-dos from all products which are connected to the SAP Task Center;
  • Gives you key insights via insights cards from products connected to SAP Start,
  • Assists you with Joule, your copilot for working with SAP.

Figure 3: SAP Start, now with direct access to product home pages. This example shows direct access to three product home pages in the “Products” section: SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Fieldglass and SAP SuccessFactors. ALT Text: Below the “Products” section the screen shows the To-Dos section, with four cards for pending tasks, the first three with Approve and Reject buttons on them. Below that, the Insights section is shown, with two rows of four cards, starting with lists of My Statements of Work, My Workers, My Team, List of Top 4 Customer Projects.

Now, SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Integrated Business Planning support SAP Start, in addition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors and SAP Fieldglass. Other cloud products can be integrated to provide tasks via SAP Task Center. You can find out how to set up and integrate cloud products to SAP Start here:

  • Documentation: SAP Start.

SAP Build Work Zone

Joule is now also generally available for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition:

  • Joule is Now Integrated into SAP Build Work Zone!

SAP Build Work Zone now allows you to integrate web UIs based on HTML5 from other SAP BTP subaccounts. This is done by creating an HTML5 business solution that acts as a content provider in the subaccount.

  • This business solution can be accessed not only by the local subaccount but also by remote subaccounts through destinations.
  • To set up an HTML5 business solution as a content provider, the provider subaccount needs to adjust the HTML5 code to include a cdm.json file, which defines the design-time business content such as roles, groups, spaces, and pages, along with destinations. Find out more here:
    • Simplifying HTML5 Content Consumption Across Subaccounts.

In my blog post from February 2024, I explained that SAP Build Work Zone allows customers to define guided experiences, i.e. wizards, to guide users step-by-step through complex processes.

A major enhancement in the October 2024 release is the ability to model guided processes directly in SAP Build Process Automation, bringing powerful process automation to your SAP Build projects. Get an overview here:

  • Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build.

Just recently, a new wizard setting was added to allow the execution of the same wizard multiple times. This setting is available both in freestyle wizards and in wizards that are based on a Guided Process from SAP Build Process Automation. For example, an admin can enable this option on the Purchase Order wizard, and this will allow users to run this wizard multiple times to purchase different items.

Enhanced Page Builder Experience across SAP Build Work Zone:

The SAP Build Work Zone page builder experience has been significantly enhanced with new features. Users can now enjoy a widget context menu, the ability to move sections up and down, and a section configuration menu, as shown in Figure 4. Additionally, the option to show or hide section headers and various accessibility improvements make the editor more user-friendly and versatile.

Figure 4: SAP Build Work Zone enhanced page builder experience. ALT Text: the image shows a section of the Work Zone screen with a visual editor. The section is partitioned into three sub-sections, each with a button “Add Widget”, the rightmost one shows an open context menu for the button, which shows that you can also add a column. The leftmost section contains a simple list of items, the middle one a bar chart for Project Cloud Revenue, the rightmost a donut chart for Open SAP Incidents.

Here is the guide with recommendations and further details:

  • How to Build and Design a Workpage.

SAP Task Center

Users can conveniently access tasks from many different systems in one place – not only from SAP products, we also provide APIs for integrating tasks from 3rd-party products.

Even more conveniently, users can now be notified of new tasks via e-mail. Also, end user notifications can now be enabled if you are using SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition as central point of entry for accessing your business applications.

SAP Build Work Zone itself can also be set up as a task provider for SAP Task Center. This means that tasks created by SAP Build Work Zone will also appear in SAP Task Center.

Furthermore, SAP Task Center now allows users to forward approvals.

New Intelligent Recommendations Feature Available for SAP Build Process Automation Tasks

We are introducing AI to expedite task processing: intelligent recommendations leverage artificial intelligence to streamline and accelerate task approval processes. They assist users in making decisions by analyzing key attributes relevant to specific business cases and generating a confidence level in percent for their tasks. Figure 5 shows some approvals with a confidence level of 100%, and some with 97.5%.

Figure 5: New Intelligent Recommendations feature in SAP Task Center. ALT Text: An image of the task center list of tasks, with a filter bar at the top with seven fields available, and the field “Task Type” set to “Manager Approval”. The list shows six entries. The new column “Confidence Level” is highlighted, and shows 100% for the first four entries, and 97.5% for the last two.

Find out more here:

  • Enhance Task Approvals with the Power of AI.

SAP Mobile Start

Beyond providing Joule natively for mobile devices, SAP Mobile Start has recently introduced a number of user experience improvements with their 2.0 and 2.1 releases. Here, I would like to highlight the support for ‘new site experience’, giving users a more structured view of applications and information.

Figure 6: SAP Mobile Start with Spaces & Pages from new site experience. ALT Text: Two images of mobile phones. On the left the Apps page, which contains “My Apps Space” showing a list of three page names “Customer Management Page”, “Project management page” and “App Page”. On the right, the App Page is shown, with a list of six apps as cards, with an icon on the left of the app name. The first three (My Inbox, Monitor Situations, Manage My Timesheet) have coloured KPI values displayed on the right.

A further significant enhancement is the support of declarative UI integration cards, which are displayed embedded in the respective pages on the Apps screen.

Figure 7: SAP Mobile Start supports UI integration cards from SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. ALT Text: Two mobile phone images. On the left Project Apps, which shows a flat card for Project Factsheet and below that a UI integration card showing overall status for active projects, with a list of five projects along with their overall status – four on track, one with “high risk” in red; at the bottom of the card a “See all” button. On the right two further UI integration cards are shown: a list of top 4 projects, and “Cost to Date – Timeline” containing a KPI number for the actual cost to date and below that a line chart of actual and planned cost per month.

For a good and satisfying user experience, being able to personalize the UI so that it fits with how you like to work is a key element. To this end, SAP Mobile Start has now introduced personalization of the start page, allowing you to rearrange the order of sections as well as hiding sections which you don’t need – take a look at it in Figure 8.

Figure 8: SAP Mobile Start personalization of the start screen. ALT Text: Four mobile phone images. On the left the start page, with the sections Insights and News visible. Next, an image of the edit screen, with five sections listed: Latest To-Dos, Favorites, Insights, App Suggestions (deselected), News. Next, an image of the five sections, all selected, now with Favorites first and Latest To-Dos second. On the right, the start page with the sections Favorites at the top (containing flat cards for apps), below that Latest To-Dos (containing larger cards for ToDos, one shocing a purchase requisition with Total Amount $799.99).

To find out more about recent innovations as well as what is planned in our road map, have a look at:

  • SAP Mobile Start V2.0 - Release Update
  • Joule on Mobile & Start Screen Personalization: SAP Mobile Start V2.1 - Release Update
  • SAP Mobile Start Road Map.

Continue Reading…

I hope you enjoyed this first part, covering cross-product UX services. Part 2 is now available:

  • SAP UX Q1/2025 Update – Part 2: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and SAP Fiori Launchpad.

I will post part three in the next few days, covering a large number of UX innovations in SAP SuccessFactors.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: Datasphere - Populating Dynamic Default Values in Analytical Model Variables
entry.content:
Populating dynamic default values in report variables without user entry is a powerful feature in reporting tools that allows the default value for a variable to be determined at runtime based on certain conditions or logic, rather than being fixed or manually specified by the report designer.

In SAP BW/4HANA, dynamic default values can be set using customer exit variables. The I_STEP variable helps define which stage of processing the exit is at, such as initialisation, selection, or validation. Typically, when I_STEP = 1, the exit is in the initialisation stage, where you can dynamically set default values based on custom ABAP code, but how do we achieve the same results in SAP Datasphere? 

In this blog, I will walk through the process of populating dynamic default values in SAP Datasphere Analytical Model variables without requiring user entry, using a combination of built-in functionality and SQL Script.

In this example, I will explain the process of setting the previous fiscal year period by default in the FISCPER analytical model standard variable.

Step-by-Step Guide: Assigning the Previous Fiscal Year Period to the FISCPER Variable dynamically.

Step 1: Create a table function with the required logic to define the dynamic variable value. This table function will be triggered in real-time during the execution of the analytical model in Datasphere.

Sample SQL Script:

 

Step 2:  Create a fiscal year period Variable in Analytical Model and select the Dynamic Default in Value Definition section. Note: I have used standard variable in this example and it is mapped to an input parameter so the default value can be passed to input parameter of downstream models. You can also user filter variables at analytical model level if you don't need input parameter at downstream models.
 

 

Step 3 :  Select your table function : Default Value Table in Lookup Entity Field and select FISCPER in Result Column Field.
 

 

Step 4:  Deploy the Analytical Model and preview the data.  Here you can see previous fiscal period 2024010 as a default value which is generated by the table function.
 

 

Step 5:  Create a SAC Story based on above analytical model, system will populate the FISCPER variable with the default value 2024010 which is generated by the table function.
 

This functionality can be implemeneted in below variables:

  1. Standard variable
  2. Filter Variable
  3. Restricted Measure Variable 
  4. Reference Date Variable.

It's a simple functionality but a powerful feature in Datasphere.

entry.title: Elevate Your SAC Dashboards: Unleash the Power of Custom R Visualizations!
entry.content:
In the world of Business Intelligence and Analytics, visual storytelling is a powerful tool for uncovering insights and driving informed decision-making. SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC), with its robust dashboarding and analytics capabilities, stands as a leader in this domain. But what if you need to go beyond its native visualizations? Enter R, the widely used programming language for statistical computing and graphics.

By combining the flexibility of R's visualization libraries with SAC's interactive dashboards, you can create custom widgets that elevate your analytics game. From dynamic heatmaps to intricate network diagrams, R empowers you to craft visuals that cater to unique business requirements, enabling advanced storytelling and better decision support through widgets, such as Gauge Charts, in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). We’ll guide you through the setup process, demonstrate a practical example using ggplot2, and share best practices for seamlessly integrating R-powered widgets into SAC dashboards. Whether you’re a data scientist, business analyst, or SAC user looking to push the boundaries of analytics, this guide will unlock new opportunities to enhance your SAC dashboards with custom, dynamic visualizations.

 Let’s dive in and transform your analytics experience!

Set Up R Integration in SAC

Before you can start creating R-powered charts, you need to ensure that SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is properly connected to your R environment. This integration is essential for running R scripts and embedding custom visualizations like Gauge Charts into your SAC dashboards.

1. Access SAC:

  • Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud account: Ensure you have the necessary access rights to create stories, dashboards, and use custom widgets in SAC.

2. Activate R Script Integration:

  • Go to the SAC Administration Page:
    • Navigate to System > Administration > Connectivity.
    • Here, check and ensure R integration is available and enabled.

 

  • Ensure the R Environment is Ready:
    1. SAC requires an external R server or R environment to execute the scripts.
    2. Make sure that R packages like ggplot2, plotly, or any other visualization libraries are installed on your R environment, as these will be used to create custom charts.

3. Verify Configuration:

  • Check Connection Details: Ensure that SAC is correctly configured to connect with the R server.
  • If possible, run a test script from SAC to confirm that the R integration is working properly.

Here’s a step-by-step guide to creating and using a custom R-Widget in your SAC Story.

Step 1: Add a Custom Widget:

  • Click on + Add or Insert in the Story workspace.
  • Select Others => R Visualization.

Step 2: On the Builder pane,

  • Choose + Add Input Data, where you can select the model from where the data needs to be used for the widget
  • You have the flexibility to choose Input from more than one model

 

  • Double click on the model name and Select the Measures and Dimensions to be used in the widget from the Builder.
  • Using the preview option(Expand the window by clicking the below icon on the top right)

 

Step 3: On the Builder pane,

  • Choose + Edit Script, where we will write the R script for Gauge meter/Taco meter
  • The Script Tab for an R Widget is divided into four sections, each serving a specific purpose to configure and execute the widget. Here's an overview of these sections
  1. Editor is where you write and manage your R script.
  2. The Environment section displays the objects (e.g., dataframes, variables) created during the script execution.
  3. Console shows real-time outputs, logs, and error messages generated by your R script.
  4. The Preview section renders the output of your R script, such as charts or visualizations.

  • On the Editor space use the R-Script shared for Gauge meter,

 

library(plotly)
library(ggplot2)
# Create the Gauge Chart using Plotly
fig <- plot_ly(
  domain = list(x = c(0, 10000), y = c(0, 10000)),  # Define the size of the plot
  value = Model1$Count,  # Current value from the data model (replace Model1$Count with actual value)
  title = list(text = "Sales Performance", font = list(size = 10)),  # Title of the gauge
  type = "indicator",  # Define the chart type as "indicator" for the gauge
  mode = "gauge+number",  # Display both the gauge and the number
  number = list(font = list(color = "black", size = 20)),  # Number formatting in the center of the gauge
  gauge = list(
    # Set the gauge axis with range limits and tick values
    axis = list(
      range = c(0, MD_XXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total),  # Define the range for the gauge axis
      tickwidth = 1,  # Set the width of ticks
      tickcolor = "darkblue",  # Set the tick color
      tickmode = "array",  # Use array for ticks
      tickvals = c(0, MD_XXXXX$`Sales Target Ct-YTD`, MD_XXXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total)  # Define the tick values
    ),
    bar = list(color = "black"),  # Set the color of the gauge bar
    steps = list(
      # Define color-coded steps for different performance ranges
      list(range = c(0, (MD_XXXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total * 30 / 100)), color = "Red"),  # Red step (below 30% target)
      list(range = c((MD_XXXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total * 30 / 100), (MD_XXXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total * 65 / 100)), color = "Yellow"),  # Yellow step (30-65% target)
      list(range = c((MD_XXXXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total * 65 / 100), MD_XXXXX$Sales_Target_Count_Total), color = "Green")  # Green step (above 65% target)
    ),
    threshold = list(
      line = list(color = "Limegreen", width = 4),  # Define the threshold line with color and width
      thickness = 0.75,  # Set the thickness of the threshold line
      value = MD_XXXXX$`Sales Target Ct-YTD`  # The threshold value (YTD sales target)
    )
  )
)

# Adjust layout (margins, etc.)
fig <- fig %>%
  layout(
    margin = list(l = 20, r = 50, t = 10, b = 10)  
  )
# Display the gauge chart
Fig

 

Step 4: After adding the script, click Run or Execute to run the R script.

  • The Gauge Meter should now be displayed with the values and colors dynamically set based on the data in the input model.

Value: The gauge will display the current value (e.g., Model1$Count) in the center of the chart.

Thresholds and Colors: Based on the Sales Target, the gauge will show color-coded sections:

  • Red for values below 30% of the target.
  • Yellow for values between 30% and 65% of the target.
  • Green for values above 65% of the target.

 Dynamic Updates: The gauge will dynamically update based on the values from the data model, ensuring that your visualizations stay accurate and up-to-date.

  •  If needed, adjust the layout, margins, and colors to match your desired visual theme.

One of the powerful features of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) is its ability to dynamically link custom R widgets like your Gauge Meter to page or story filters, as well as perform linked analysis from other tables or charts. This makes the widget even more interactive and responsive to user input.

By the end of this blog, you will have a clear understanding of how to integrate R-powered visualizations like Gauge meter into your SAC dashboards, and how to leverage the interactive features like filters and linked analysis. This will enable you to create more dynamic, user-driven dashboards that provide deeper insights and a more engaging analytics experience.

entry.title: Deliver AI value across your business with DataRobot Insight Apps for SAP
entry.content:
At DataRobot, we’ve worked with hundreds of clients across industries and one thing remains crystal clear: delivering AI to where users need it, and where they’ll actually use it, is still a challenge. As organizations work to fit AI into their business processes, they’re still running into the same big hurdles. 

Organizations struggle with unifying data and getting it AI-ready, and they struggle with building AI solutions that solve pressing business problems, and delivering those solutions to users in time to make a real impact. Instead of focusing on what needs to be delivered, they get bogged down with making data accessible, creating the infrastructure for AI deployment, and designing solutions.

With the launch of SAP Business Data Cloud, and the AI-powered Insight Apps for SAP from DataRobot, it’s never been easier to move past infrastructure constraints, and focus on delivering real business value from AI. 

This joint solution provides the fastest way to unify data access, operationalize AI, and deliver AI where it’s needed – transforming how organizations work with AI.

Enhancing SAP Business Data Cloud with Insight Apps 

SAP Business Data Cloud unifies data and analytics with companies’ business processes, empowering leaders with complete context for faster decision making and better business planning. This serves as a trusted data foundation, delivering seamless access to reliable, business-ready data and empowering organizations to prepare data for AI and drive innovation with confidence. 

To help organizations leverage this unified data and rapidly deliver relevant and impactful AI solutions, DataRobot is launching AI-powered Insight Apps for Business Data Cloud. 

These new application suites combine deep industry domain experience and best practices with pre-built and customizable solutions that accelerate use cases for planning, enterprise analytics, advanced analytical apps, and predictive forecasting. 

This enables SAP customers to prioritize business needs and deliver solutions built with trusted methods, and end-to-end observability and oversight. 

DataRobot Insight Apps provide the fastest way to confidently develop and deliver critical AI use cases directly where they’re needed.

Each DataRobot Insight App comes complete with the key components to tackle critical use cases:

  • Multiple specialized, pre-built and customizable solutions tailored to specific industry vertical or horizontal use cases such as cash flow forecasting or inventory management
  • One-click integrations with data sources like Business Data Cloud and others, reducing the effort needed to connect systems
  • Proprietary AI logic built on a decade of AI experience, designed for quick deployment and accuracy at scale
  • A user-centric design that can be deployed as fully customized applications or integrated into existing systems like SAP Analytics Cloud or Integrated Business Planning.

Empowering finance with AI-powered insights 

Finance teams face enormous pressure to make accurate, data-driven decisions in an environment of increasing complexity and uncertainty. They must analyze vast amounts of data to forecast outcomes, guide strategic planning, and optimize financial operations. 

To be truly effective, these insights need to be accurate at scale and delivered in time for the business to act.

The DataRobot Insight App for Finance bridges the gap between raw data and actionable insights through seamless integration with SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud, unlocking and optimizing critical use cases across the office of finance. 

This means finance teams can integrate AI-driven forecasts directly into their finance operations, enabling teams to swiftly transform disparate and complex data into reliable insights, leading to faster and more confident decision-making. 

By augmenting critical tasks such as revenue forecasting, budget analysis, and cash flow management, teams can accelerate the delivery of impactful AI while reducing operational costs and risk. 

With ready-to-use solutions for use cases like cash flow forecasting, treasury teams can rapidly deploy solutions that enable end users to access higher quality, invoice-level forecasts precisely where they need them, providing deeper insights into cash inflows and outflows for both the short term and long term. 

This ensures finance leaders can start making more accurate operational cash management decisions in a matter of weeks. Once they have one solution in production, they can quickly scale across the department and the organization. 

Optimizing supply chains with AI-driven forecasting 

Supply chain teams face similar pressure to their FP&A counterparts, as they work to use forecasting to minimize disruption, navigate uncertainty, and ensure operational efficiency. Achieving accuracy at scale and across geographies becomes increasingly difficult as the number of products and stores grows, often requiring dozens or even hundreds of forecasts. 

Even more difficult is ensuring predictions arrive in time to take action and remain consistent with other forecasts. 

The Insight App for supply chain from DataRobot addresses core challenges across the supply chain by combining automated model development with streamlined configuration and process governance. With built-in one-click deployment, these solutions can be seamlessly infused into existing planning workflows in IBP or surfaced in SAC dashboards. This accelerates the first step for our customers to incorporate the latest AI technology, and build a more sustainable and resilient supply chain.

How a DataRobot transformed a global brewery’s supply chain

When the COVID-19 pandemic struck, one of the world’s largest breweries encountered a significant challenge. Their hand-coded models for predicting demand across 20+ markets became ineffective, leading to an imbalance in supply. While beer was flying off retail shelves, it sat untouched in bars and restaurants, costing the company $110 million annually.

The traditional hand-coded approach not only lacked accuracy but also required six months to implement for each use case, making it impossible to scale solutions in time to address the crisis. To overcome this, the brewery turned to DataRobot’s AI capabilities to better connect business data and streamline processes.

By leveraging DataRobot, the brewery reduced model deployment time from six months to just two, rapidly scaling the solution across six global markets. In just five weeks, these advanced models generated over $17 million in additional beer sales, transforming their global supply chain and ensuring they could meet demand with precision.

Put business-ready AI into the hands of your users 

The next era of AI will be defined by delivering AI where users work and how they work. Whether you’re a current SAP customer or just beginning your AI journey, the future of AI value lies in solving business problems and delivering those solutions exactly where they’re needed.

SAP and DataRobot have been focused on removing barriers to this process by helping you simplify access to your data and streamline the delivering of AI apps into the SAP products your teams use every day.

Interested to learn more? Check out DataRobot’s launch webinar featuring SAP.

We also recently published our survey that provides an in-depth view of the hurdles that are preventing AI teams from delivering value.

entry.title: 3 Reasons to Register for the SAP Business Data Cloud Primer in 30 Minutes Webinar
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business environment, leaders need real-time insights to drive better decisions and stay competitive. However, fragmented data, disconnected systems, and manual reporting make it difficult to get a clear, actionable view of your business.

If you’re looking to unlock the full potential of your data, mark your calendars for SAP’s upcoming webinar, SAP Business Data Cloud Primer in 30 Minutes. Join us for an introduction to SAP Business Data Cloud, where you’ll discover how it unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data, providing a trusted and harmonized data foundation that gives you full business context to make more impactful decisions across all lines of business.  

Here are the top three reasons to register.

1. Discover how SAP Business Data Cloud Unleashes Transformative Insights Across Lines of Business

In this webinar, you’ll gain a clear understanding of how SAP Business Data Cloud helps organizations harmonize data from SAP and non-SAP systems, delivering a single source of truth for decision-making. Learn how SAP Business Data Cloud connects data across finance, HR, supply chain, and more, to drive smarter insights and automation while maintaining business context.

2. Explore Real-World Use Cases for SAP Business Data Cloud

Hear how your business can leverage SAP Business Data Cloud to eliminate data silos, improve analytics, and enable AI-driven decision-making. Gain insights into how organizations can successfully unify their data landscapes to bridge the gap between IT and business leaders, increase efficiency, enhance operational visibility, and drive measurable business outcomes.

3. See SAP Business Data Cloud in Action

Experience a product demo showcasing SAP Business Data Cloud’s capabilities, including prebuilt analytical apps and real-time data products. See firsthand how it simplifies complex data challenges, enhances governance, and enables seamless AI-driven insights across your organization.

Secure Your Spot Today

Don’t miss this opportunity to explore how SAP Business Data Cloud can revolutionize the way your organization leverages data. Join us for the SAP Business Data Cloud Primer in 30 Minutes webinar on March 13. to gain the knowledge and tools needed to accelerate your data-driven transformation.

entry.title: SAP BTP: “Change Agent” or “Scale Agent” Part 7 – How to select Data & Analytics BTP Services
entry.content:
SAP Business Technology Platform offers a robust suite of over 80 services, many of which play critical roles in supporting enterprise-level operations. The challenge lies in determining the right combination of services tailored to specific use cases. Crafting an effective SAP BTP solution requires a deep understanding of the platform. While established architectural patterns provide a solid foundation, assessing individual use cases to identify the most suitable architecture is a skill that requires experience and strategic evaluation.

 

How to decide when to use what services in SAP BTP

 

To effectively choose the right services, it is essential to understand the core archetypes within SAP BTP, which include:

  • Application Modernization
  • Data Modernization
  • AI & Generative AI

 

The first step in determining the appropriate services is to classify your application as either transactional or analytical. Transactional applications typically align with the Application Modernization archetype, while analytical applications are categorized under Data Modernization. This classification serves as the foundation for building an application on SAP BTP.

 

Once the core archetype is defined, further enhancements can be incorporated, particularly with AI or Generative AI services, which fall under the AI & Gen AI archetype. For a broader understanding of the classification, please refer to Figure 1.

 

 

Figure 1 – Determining application as Transactional or Analytical

 

After identifying the correct use-case type, you can begin designing the appropriate architecture. This step is crucial for determining which SAP BTP services are essential for implementing and optimizing the solution.

 

Data Modernization archetype

 

Applications categorized as analytical fall under the Data Modernization archetype, highlighting their role in transforming and enhancing data processes and infrastructure. This categorization is essential as it aids organizations in identifying and leveraging technological advancements to optimize data management and utilization.

 

Figure 3 below provides a decision flow to further illustrate this classification and guide stakeholders in understanding and applying these principles effectively. By adhering to these frameworks, businesses can enhance their data processing capabilities, ensuring more efficient and insightful data-driven decision-making.

 

 

Figure 3 – Data Modernization archetype decision matrix

 

Step-1 – Business Intelligence

 

Ask if it's a standard report or dashboard accessed from a single source; if yes, use SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

Creating non-standard custom reports in SAP S/4HANA and extending them with SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) can greatly enhance your reporting capabilities. Here are some examples where custom reports can be developed:

 

Application Type

Finance

Sales, Service & Marketing

Procurement & Expense Management

Supply Chain

Human Resources

Operations

IT

Multi source Financial Analysis Report

X

 

 

 

 

 

 

Procurement and Supplier Performance Dashboard

 

 

X

 

 

 

 

Sales Pipeline & Revenue Forecast Report

X

X

 

 

 

 

 

Manufacturing Efficiency & Downtime Analysis

 

 

 

 

 

X

 

Customer Profitability and Segmentation Report

X

X

 

 

 

 

 

Environment, Social & Governance (ESG) Report

X

 

X

X

X

X

X

Consolidated Budget vs Actuals Report

X

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • **Multi-Source Financial Analysis Report**:

   - **Objective**: Aggregate financial data from different source systems or modules like FI, CO, and external databases.

   - **Features**: Real-time currency conversion, dynamic profit center hierarchy adjustments, and custom KPIs.

   - **SAC Integration**: Use SAC’s Visualization tools for dynamic dashboards and predictive analytics to forecast financial trends.

  • **Procurement and Supplier Performance Dashboard**:

   - **Objective**: Evaluate and monitor supplier performance in real-time, integrating data from procurement, quality management, and external vendor ratings.

   - **Features**: Supplier scorecards, lead time analysis, and quality non-conformance rates.

   - **SAC Integration**: Create interactive dashboards with scorecards and trend analyses to manage supplier risks and opportunities.

 

  • **Sales Pipeline and Revenue Forecast Report**:

   - **Objective**: Provide insights into sales pipeline status and revenue projections.

   - **Features**: Sales cycle analysis, probability-adjusted forecast, and win/loss ratios.

   - **SAC Integration**: Use predictive analytics to forecast sales and identify the highest impact leads using machine learning models.

 

  • **Manufacturing Efficiency and Downtime Analysis**:

   - **Objective**: Track production efficiency and downtime to improve operational performance.

   - **Features**: Machine-level efficiency reports, downtime causes analysis, and OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) calculation.

   - **SAC Integration**: Use SAC to visualize production KPIs and create automated alerts for operator action.

 

  • **Customer Profitability and Segmentation Report**:

   - **Objective**: Analyze customer data to understand profitability and identify high-value segments.

   - **Features**: Profitability scorecards, churn analysis, and segment-based profit comparisons.

   - **SAC Integration**: Utilize SAC for customer profitability analysis with segment drill-downs and advanced data visualizations.

 

  • **Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) Report**:

   - **Objective**: Compile and analyze ESG metrics for sustainability reporting.

   - **Features**: Carbon footprint analysis, diversity metrics, and compliance tracking.

   - **SAC Integration**: Leverage SAC to create comprehensive ESG dashboards with benchmarks and performance indicators.

 

  • **Consolidated Budget vs. Actuals Report**:

   - **Objective**: Compare budgeted figures against actuals across various organizational units.

   - **Features**: Variance analysis, periodic trends, and multi-level budget hierarchy reports.

   - **SAC Integration**: Use SAC’s planning capabilities to simulate future budget scenarios and variances.

 

To create these custom reports, collaboration between business analysts, report developers, and subject matter experts is essential. SAC’s capabilities in data connectivity, visualization, and machine learning provide a robust platform to build and deploy these tools effectively. Always ensure data integrity and security when handling sensitive business information across these platforms.

 

Step-2 – Business Data Fabric

Consider if data from multiple sources is needed; if yes, employ SAP Datasphere for a unified enterprise data view.

Cocreating a data fabric infused with context and governance for true business-wide data-driven decision-making

 

 

X

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Datasphere, formerly known as SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, is a comprehensive data management solution designed to integrate, manage, and analyze data across an enterprise. With its capabilities, businesses can build various kinds of applications and solutions, including:

 

  • **Data Warehousing Applications**:

   - Centralize data from disparate sources for more accessible reporting and analytics.

   - Create a unified view of organizational data to streamline business intelligence processes.

 

  1. **Business Analytics Applications**:

   - Develop robust analytical dashboards and reports using data models and visualization tools.

   - Leverage prebuilt connectors for SAP and non-SAP sources for comprehensive insights.

 

  • **Data Integration Solutions**:

   - Integrate and harmonize data from various sources, such as on-premise databases, cloud services, and external systems.

   - Establish real-time data pipelines to ensure up-to-date insights.

 

  • **Financial and Operational Planning**:

   - Implement planning solutions that integrate financial, sales, and operational data for comprehensive financial forecasting and analysis.

   - Facilitate scenario planning and what-if analysis.

 

  • **Predictive and Advanced Analytics**:

   - Build predictive models using integrated Machine Learning services to forecast trends and behaviors.

   - Use advanced analytics to derive deeper insights from structured and unstructured data.

 

  • **Contextualized Data Access**:

   - Provide tailored data access to various business units, ensuring relevant insights are easily accessible.

   - Enhance decision-making by delivering contextualized data in real-time.

 

  • **Data-Driven Applications**:

   - Develop applications that consume and display data insights directly to end-users, integrating analytics into business processes.

   - Create SaaS offerings for clients that leverage their data for enhanced productivity and operations.

 

  • **IoT and Big Data Analytics**:

   - Harness IoT data by integrating with SAP’s IoT solutions and processing large volumes of data efficiently.

   - Analyze big data sets to extract actionable insights for operational improvements.

 

  • **Customer and Supply Chain Insights**:

   - Develop customer analytics solutions to improve customer experience through insights on behavior and preferences.

   - Optimize supply chain operations with data insights into logistics, inventory, and supplier performance.

 

The flexibility and scalability of SAP Datasphere allow businesses to innovate and quickly adapt to changing data landscapes, thereby fostering data-driven decision-making across the organization.

 

Step-3 – Predictive Analytics

Determine if predictive capabilities are necessary; if yes, add HANA Cloud to your data platform and business intelligence.

 

SAP HANA Cloud's predictive capabilities can be applied across various lines of business (LOB) to provide valuable insights and enhance decision-making processes. Here are some common use cases by LOB:

### Finance

  1. **Cash Flow Forecasting**: Predict cash flow to optimize liquidity and financial planning.
  2. **Fraud Detection**: Identify unusual patterns that may indicate fraudulent activities.
  3. **Credit Risk Assessment**: Evaluate the creditworthiness of customers and predict potential defaults.
  4. **Expense Management**: Analyze expense patterns to forecast future costs and optimize budgets.

 

### Marketing

  1. **Customer Segmentation**: Identify distinct customer groups based on purchasing behaviors and preferences.
  2. **Churn Prediction**: Predict which customers are likely to leave and design retention strategies.
  3. **Campaign Effectiveness**: Analyze past campaign performances to predict the success of future initiatives.
  4. **Market Basket Analysis**: Understand product associations and optimize cross-selling and up-selling strategies.

 

### Sales

  1. **Demand Forecasting**: Predict future sales volumes to optimize inventory and supply chain operations.
  2. **Lead Scoring**: Rank leads based on their likelihood to convert to closed deals.
  3. **Revenue Forecasting**: Estimate future revenue based on historical data and market trends.
  4. **Sales Pipeline Analysis**: Predict the closure of deals and optimize sales strategies.

 

### Human Resources

  1. **Employee Turnover Prediction**: Forecast employee attrition and identify factors contributing to turnover.
  2. **Talent Acquisition**: Predict the success of job candidates based on past hiring data.
  3. **Skill Gap Analysis**: Identify gaps in employee skills and predict future training needs.
  4. **Workforce Planning**: Model future workforce requirements based on business growth scenarios.

 

### Supply Chain

  1. **Inventory Optimization**: Predict inventory levels to reduce carrying costs and prevent stockouts.
  2. **Demand Planning**: Forecast demand fluctuations and align supply chain operations accordingly.
  3. **Supplier Risk Assessment**: Evaluate risks associated with suppliers based on historical performance.
  4. **Logistics Optimization**: Predict logistics bottlenecks and optimize transportation routes.

 

### Production

  1. **Predictive Maintenance**: Forecast machinery breakdowns and plan maintenance activities proactively.
  2. **Quality Control**: Predict defects and improve quality control processes.
  3. **Capacity Planning**: Forecast production needs to optimize capacity utilization.
  4. **Waste Reduction**: Identify patterns contributing to waste and optimize processes for sustainability.

 

### IT and Operations

  1. **System Outage Prediction**: Predict and prevent IT system failures and downtimes.
  2. **Resource Optimization**: Forecast IT resource needs to optimize infrastructure and reduce costs.
  3. **Security Threat Analysis**: Predict and prevent potential cybersecurity threats.
  4. **Process Automation**: Use predictive insights to enhance

 

Step-4 – Planning

Evaluate if planning capabilities are required; if yes, include SAP Analytics Cloud planning solutions with your data platform and business intelligence.

These use cases highlight the flexibility of SAP Analytics Cloud Planning in addressing the specific needs and objectives of various departments within an organization, enabling integrated planning and informed decision-making across the enterprise.

 

Step-5 – Custom Reporting or Analytics

For needs not covered above, consider a mix of SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Datasphere, SAP Data Intelligence, and other SAP BTP services.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud and Planning can be applied to a variety of complex and nuanced use cases across different lines of business (LOBs).
