X

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • **Advanced Supply Chain Planning:**

   - **Demand Sensing and Shaping:** Use predictive analytics to enhance demand forecasting with real-time data inputs like social media trends, weather patterns, and economic indicators.

   - **Integrated Business Planning (IBP):** Complex scenarios where finance, sales, and operations planning are integrated to optimize inventory levels and production schedules.

 

  • **Human Capital Analytics:**

   - **Talent Retention and Development Strategy:** By analyzing employee performance, engagement surveys, and external benchmark data, the tool can help predict turnover risk and formulate talent development strategies.

   - **Diversity and Inclusion Impact Analysis:** Assess the effectiveness of diversity initiatives on organizational performance through scenario modeling.

 

  • **Complex Financial Planning and Analysis:**

   - **Scenario-based Risk Management:** Running multiple stress-test scenarios to assess financial risks under various economic conditions with real-time data adjustments.

   - **Strategic Workforce Planning linked with Financials:** Integration of workforce cost planning with long-term financial forecasts to assess the strategic implications of workforce decisions.

 

  • **High-Tech and R&D Portfolio Optimization:**

   - **R&D Investment Valuation:** Simulate and forecast the potential return on investment for various R&D projects using historical data and predictive analytics.

   - **Lifecycle Analytics for Product Innovation:** Analyze the entire lifecycle from ideation to market penetration and end-of-life, optimizing resources and timing for product release.

 

  • **Complex Manufacturing Process Analytics:**

   - **Predictive Maintenance with IoT Data:** Utilizing IoT sensor data to predict equipment failures and optimize maintenance schedules.

   - **Production Yield Optimization:** Analyzing the various parameters that impact production quality and yield, and implementing scenario planning to increase efficiency.

 

  • **Cross-Industry Sustainability and Regulatory Reporting:**

   - **Carbon Footprint and Environmental Impact Analysis:** Complex modeling involving environmental data to forecast carbon footprint and align with sustainability targets.

   - **Compliance and Risk Management Reporting:** Dynamic modeling of regulatory changes and their impact on business operations and financial performance.

 

  • **Retail and Consumer Behavior Analytics:**

   - **Omni-channel Strategy Optimization:** Evaluating customer touchpoints and optimizing marketing spend across various channels to maximize ROI.

   - **Personalized Promotions and Pricing Strategies:** Advanced analytics to tailor promotions at an individual customer level based on purchase history and predictive behavior analysis.

 

  • **Smart Infrastructure and Urban Planning:**

   - **   :** Planning and analyzing the long-term sustainability and cost implications of infrastructure projects.

   - **Urban Mobility and Transportation Forecasting:** Visualizing and forecasting urban travel patterns to improve city planning and public transport efficiency.

 

These complex use cases require a deep integration of multiple data sources, sophisticated analytical models, and cross-functional collaboration, often involving advanced features of SAP Analytics Cloud like predictive analytics, machine learning, and integration with other SAP and non-SAP systems.

 

 

Conclusion

 

In conclusion, designing an optimal SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) solution requires a thorough understanding of the platform’s diverse services and their application to specific business use-cases. By carefully evaluating whether an application is transactional or analytical, businesses can select the appropriate archetype—such as Application Modernization, Data Modernization, or AI & Generative AI—ensuring that the right SAP BTP services are deployed to meet their needs.

 

The platform offers a range of services, from SAP Analytics Cloud for reporting and planning to SAP Datasphere for data foundation and SAP HANA Cloud for predictive analytics and vector engine. By leveraging these tools, organizations can enhance their data processing capabilities, improve decision-making with advanced analytics, and foster innovation across their operations. Customization of reporting, data integration, and predictive capabilities enables businesses to drive more efficient, insightful, and forward-thinking outcomes, establishing a strong foundation for both present needs and future growth.

 

Thanks for your time in reading this blog, will see you in the next blog in this series SAP BTP from “Change Agent” to “Scale Agent”.

entry.title: SAP Analytics Cloud File Repository API
entry.content:
The Q1 2025 release of SAP Analytics Cloud includes amongst its new features the File Repository API, which provides developers with programmatic access to metadata about all the objects stored in the Files area of SAP Analytics Cloud.

This API can be useful if you're building your own application that needs access to metadata about files stored in SAP Analytics Cloud. Use-cases include:

  • Embedding lists of links to SAP Analytics Cloud content in your application
  • Retrieving metadata from multiple SAP Analytics Cloud tenants to compare their contents
  • Retrieving metadata for external cataloging purposes
  • Retrieving metadata for other administrative/housekeeping purposes

The API includes two endpoints:

  • /api/v1/filerepository/Resources is the main API endpoint that you use to query for file resources. You can also query for a specific resource by resourceId as in /api/v1/filerepository/Resources(‘11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29’)
  • /api/v1/filerepository/$metadata describes the entities that are exposed by the API

Sample Results

For each file resource returned by the API, the default set of properties will be similar to what’s shown in the following example:

 

    {
      "resourceId": "11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29",
      "objectId": "STORY:t.1:11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29",
      "name": "Name of the file",
      "description": "Description of the file",
      "resourceType": "STORY",
      "resourceSubtype": "",
      "createdTime": "2025-01-16T22:28:23.538Z",
      "createdBy": "USER001",
      "modifiedTime": "2025-01-16T22:28:23.538Z",
      "modifiedBy": "USER001",
      "folderType": "PUBLIC",
      "parentFolderResourceId": "E3C80C0224E39749AB1B831D57408FC6",
      "workspaceId": null,
      "workspaceName": null,
      "openURL": "/sap/fpa/ui/tenants/b1567d/bo/story/11500004B0B75677E705DD8C9801AC29",
      "isMobile": true,
      "isTranslationEnabled": false,
      "isFeatured": false
    }

 

 

Authentication

Since the API today only supports GET requests, you can preview its functionality using just your web browser. Log on to SAP Analytics Cloud with your usual user account, and then go to https://your-SAC-tenant-URL/api/v1/filerepository/Resources to get an initial set of results.

To use the API programmatically, you first need to create an OAuth client in your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant. Then you need to authenticate from your application to SAP Analytics Cloud using that OAuth client. SAP Analytics Cloud supports several OAuth authentication flows. For details, see Setting up Authentication | SAP Help Portal.

A straightforward authentication flow is the OAuth client credentials grant type outlined in Basic Authentication Against the Token Service. If you take that approach, first create your OAuth client under System > Administration > App Integration, setting its API Access to “File Repository Read”, and its Authorization Grant to “Client Credentials”, as shown here:

 

Authorization to File Resources

By default, the API takes into account the SAP Analytics Cloud sharing permissions of the user that is making the API request. So, if you log on to SAP Analytics Cloud and then browse the API endpoint with your web browser at /api/v1/filerepository/Resources, the query results will include all files that are available to your user account in the Files > My Files view of the product UI, plus the files stored in any workspaces that your user belongs to.

If you have an administrative account in SAP Analytics Cloud—more specifically, if you are assigned a role that includes the Manage privilege on Public Files and/or Private Files—then you can alternatively have the API return all files that are available on the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant under the Files > System view in the product UI. To achieve this administrative view via the API, append the additional URL parameter applyManagePrivilege=true to the API URL (as in, /api/v1/filerepository/Resources?applyManagePrivilege=true).  

Note: If your application authenticates with the OAuth client credentials flow, then this URL parameter applyManagePrivilege=true can similarly be used to access an administrative view of all files on the SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

 

Pagination and Total Results Count

The API paginates its results in batches of 50 resources. If your query results exceed 50 resources, then only the first 50 are initially returned, and the results body will include an "@odata.nextLink" annotation with a URL to follow to retrieve the next batch of results.

If you want to know how many total results your query has, you can include the $count=true parameter in your query URL.

The following example shows partial query results from the query URL /api/v1/filerepository/Resources?$count=true, including a total count of query results (i.e., 177), and a link to the second batch of results (i.e., “Resources?%24count=true&%24skiptoken=50”):

 

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Resources",
  "@odata.count": 177,
  "@odata.nextLink": "Resources?%24count=true&%24skiptoken=50",
  "value": [
       // metadata for the first 50 resources will appear here
   ]
}

 

To retrieve the second batch of results, you would go to /api/v1/filerepository/Resources?%24count=true&%24skiptoken=50. The results of that query will again include another @odata.nextLink annotation providing a link to the third batch of results. This will continue until you retrieve the last batch of results, at which point there will no longer be an @odata.nextLink included in the results.

 

Selecting, Sorting and Filtering

You can use the OData $select, $orderby and $filter query options to refine the results returned by the API. You can use these query options in conjunction with any of the properties that are returned for each resource by the API (e.g., resourceId, objectId, name, description, resourceType, resourceSubtype, createdTime, createdBy, etc.)

Use $select to reduce the number of properties returned by the API and thus reduce the size of its payload. For example, Resources?$select=resourceId,name,openURL will return just the resourceId, name, and openURL properties for each file resource.

Use $orderby to sort results. For example:

  • Resources?$orderby=name%20asc will sort by name in ascending order.
  • Resources?$orderby=modifiedTime%20desc will sort by modifiedTime in descending order.

Use $filter to restrict query results to just the files that match your filter criteria. For example:

  • Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'FOLDER' returns just the folder resources
  • Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'STORY' and resourceSubtype eq '' returns just the stories (where resourceSubtype is empty).
  • Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'STORY' and resourceSubtype eq 'INSIGHT' returns just the Data Analyzer Insights.
  • Resources?$filter=modifiedTime gt 2024-07-31T12:29:52Z returns just the files modified after a specific timestamp. Note that no quotation marks are needed around the timestamp value.

The following are valid filter comparison operators:

  • eq: equal
  • ne: not equal
  • gt: greater than
  • ge: greater than or equal
  • lt: less than
  • le: less than or equal
  • in: is a member of a list specified as (‘Member1’, ’Member2’)

You can combine filters and sort expressions to further refine the API query results, for example combining them as in Resources?$filter=resourceType eq 'FOLDER' and modifiedTime gt 2024-07-31T12:29:52Z&$orderby=modifiedTime desc

 

Expansion of Navigation Properties

You can retrieve additional metadata about each file resource by using the OData $expand query option.

For example, you can expand ancestorFolders to see a collection of the folders that each returned resource is contained within: Resources?$expand=ancestorFolders

Or you can expand dependencyObjects to see a collection of the file repository resources that each returned resource depends upon: Resources?$expand=dependencyObjects

You can further combine the use of the $expand query option with the OData “any” operator to iterate over dependencyObjects or ancestorFolders collections to do things like the following:

  • Retrieve all objects that depend on a specific object (based on its resourceId): Resources?$expand=dependencyObjects&$filter=dependencyObjects/any(d:d/dependencyResourceId eq 'C41qg8uuay285i6wfudm5kga9s')
  • Retrieve all objects that have an ancestor folder matching a specific name: Resources?$expand=ancestorFolders&$filter=ancestorFolders/any(a:a/name eq 'SAP_FI_FPA_S4_Financial_Planning')

Finally, you can expand all available navigation attributes at once to retrieve all possible metadata from the API: Resources?$expand=ancestorFolders,dependencyObjects,extendedProperties,extraProperty,usage,resourceEffectivePermissions

 

More Information

For more information about the File Repository API, see its documentation in the SAP Analytics Cloud REST API guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/14cac91febef464dbb1efce20e3f1613/132338a059c741209c910770227afb82.html.

entry.title: Futuristic Proposals: Elevating SAP Sales Cloud V2 with SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
As enterprises continue to demand real‑time insights and seamless data integration, SAP Sales Cloud V2 is evolving to meet these challenges. Looking forward, integrating the powerful capabilities of SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) can drive transformative change—making Sales Cloud V2 not only smarter but also more agile and secure.

1. Real‑Time Data Products for Instant Insights

BDC’s data products are pre‑curated, governed datasets that capture critical business information in real time. By integrating these with SAP Sales Cloud V2, sales teams can access:

  • Live Customer Data: Instantly view the most current customer interactions and behaviors.
  • Dynamic Pipeline Visibility: Leverage continuously updated sales opportunity data to drive faster decision‑making.

Such real‑time insights help sales teams anticipate customer needs and react swiftly to market changes, ensuring that decisions are always data‑driven.

2. Enhanced AI and Predictive Analytics

BDC’s integration with cutting‑edge AI platforms, including Databricks, enables the creation of advanced insight apps that run predictive models on unified data sets. For example:

  • Predictive Sales Forecasting: AI‑driven models can analyze historical and current sales data to forecast trends and identify high‑value opportunities.
  • Next‑Best Action Recommendations: Sales representatives receive real‑time suggestions based on customer behavior and market dynamics.

These capabilities are already being showcased in recent SAP announcements, where AI insights are turbocharging business decision‑making citeturn0search15.

3. Unified Customer Data Ecosystem

Integrating BDC with Sales Cloud V2 provides a unified, single‑version-of‑the‑truth for customer data:

  • Consistent Data Governance: With BDC’s robust versioning and data governance, sales data remains accurate and secure across multiple systems.
  • Seamless Integration Across SAP Solutions: Whether it’s data from SAP S/4HANA, SAP Ariba, or third‑party sources, BDC harmonizes disparate data into one accessible ecosystem citeturn0search8.

This unified approach reduces data silos and ensures that sales strategies are built on reliable, high‑quality data.

4. Customizable and Adaptive Insight Apps

BDC’s framework supports the development of both standard and custom insight apps:

  • Tailored Sales Dashboards: Pre‑built templates and customizable apps enable sales managers to monitor KPIs, pipeline health, and performance metrics.
  • Low-Code/No-Code Enhancements: Leveraging platforms like SAP Build, organizations can quickly adapt the solution to meet unique business requirements without heavy development overhead citeturn0search17.

Such flexibility ensures that as market needs evolve, your sales cloud remains agile and responsive.

5. Automated Data Governance and Security

As sales data becomes increasingly valuable, securing it is paramount. BDC’s advanced data governance features can:

  • Automate Compliance: Enforce data protection standards and automate data lifecycle management.
  • Ensure Data Integrity: Use semantic versioning and controlled updates to protect the integrity of sales data.

This foundation of trust and security is critical for organizations operating in regulated industries.

6. Future‑Proof Architecture

Integrating BDC with SAP Sales Cloud V2 offers a forward‑looking, microservices‑based architecture:

  • Scalability and Flexibility: The cloud‑native design allows the system to scale effortlessly, accommodating growing data volumes and evolving business needs.
  • Continuous Innovation: With BDC’s ability to rapidly deploy new AI models and insight apps, organizations can continually innovate without disruption.

This future‑proof architecture not only supports current requirements but also positions your sales cloud to adapt to emerging trends and technologies.

Conclusion

By harnessing the power of SAP Business Data Cloud, SAP Sales Cloud V2 can be transformed into a truly best‑in‑class solution. Real‑time data products, AI‑driven insights, a unified customer data ecosystem, customizable apps, automated governance, and a future‑proof architecture collectively enable sales teams to work smarter, faster, and more securely. As organizations look to gain a competitive edge in an ever‑evolving market, integrating BDC with Sales Cloud V2 is a strategic move toward a data‑driven, agile future.

Embrace these futuristic proposals and unlock the full potential of your sales organization.

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2025 年第一季度更新介绍
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能（BI）、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和数据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。 

本文介绍 SAP 分析云 2025 年第一季度的季度发布 (QRC) 的新功能和增强功能列表。 

在链接的视频中，SAP 产品专家将向您展示 2025 年第一季度的部分重点功能。 

 

 

系统和服务管理 

  • 能够直观地区分不同租户

 

面向计划和分析的人工智能 

  • 一问即得：即可获取以不同货币显示的结果答案

 

数据分析器 ​

  • 制定洞察计划
  • 实现针对表格的附加拖放功能

 

数据集成 

  • 支持克隆现有的加载作业
  • 支持在导入连接中使用 SAP Business Warehouse（BW）的消息服务器

 

​企业计划  

  • 实现与 SAP Datasphere 的“无缝计划”集成
  • 指南针 – 实现基于蒙特卡洛算法的风险分析模拟
  • 数据操作 - 自动生成计划范围
  • 数据操作 – 支持激活和取消数据操作步骤
  • 验证规则 – 支持激活和取消验证规则
  • 作业监控器 - 支持多步操作
  • 计划输入 – 添加 onAfterDataEntryProcess() 表格事件
  • 计划流程管理 – 模板查看器
  • 计划流程管理 – 支持将 Excel 加载项报表作为工作文件

 

Microsoft Office 集成  

  • 在 Microsoft Excel 中实现与 SAP Datasphere 的“无缝计划”集成
  • 将加载项工作簿添加到日历任务的工作文件中
  • 在Microsoft Excel 设计器窗格和对话框中新增了插图
  • 支持在 Microsoft Excel 中完全重置表格
  • 支持 Microsoft Excel 中的表格格式化

 

故事设计

  • 提供在鼠标悬停于表格数据单元格上时，打开或关闭“阈值”工具提示的选项
  • 在“与其他排名”功能中提供更多细节，但不包括 SAP Business Warehouse（BW）和表格
  • 表格：能够将度量与 SAP BW 和 SAP S/4HANA 阈值定义中的度量进行比较
  • 阈值：度量之间的比较（适用于 SAP BW）
  • 故事计算中支持单位转换
  • 垂直过滤器面板：适用于图表和表格

 

故事可扩展性

  • 支持组合微件的全局计算
  • 支持在组合微件中使用流式布局面板和滑块微件
  • 支持将组合微件作为链接分析的目标
  • 添加在组合微件中禁用组合模型配置的选项
  • 能够在故事 URL 参数中更新公开的脚本变量
  • 允许使用脚本 API 定制动态弹出窗口大小
  • 增强了分层输入控件的脚本 API
  • 实现用于动态更改图表数据模型的脚本 API
  • 自定义微件：支持显示度量的用户标识和描述信息
  • 自定义微件：支持在生成器面板中显示总计操作

 

故事演示 

  • 视频数据故事能够创建简短的视频进而呈现业务洞察

 

用户协作 

  • 模型中的注释管理，允许用户预览与模型相关的注释内容

 

本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和改进功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP 知识库文章。 

如果您对本博文的内容有任何疑问，可以在下方发表评论。如果您有具体的产品或技术问题，请在我们的中文 SAP Analytics Cloud 问答论坛上提问。访问我们的 SAP Analytics Cloud 社区页面，了解更多产品信息、卓越实践等。查看我们的 SAP 路线图浏览器，了解 SAP 分析云即将推出的更多功能。

entry.title: SAP 分析云经典模型和故事迁移指南
entry.content:
由于SAP 分析云经典故事计划将于2026年第二季度QRC 更新后停用（相关博客），我们鼓励所有SAP 分析云租户中仍存在大量经典故事和模型的客户开始计划并尝试将经典故事迁移至优化故事的同时，也将经典模型迁移至新模型这一话题纳入考量。虽然SAP 分析云中提供了一些迁移工具，但考虑到实际业务环境的复杂性，我们建议您为迁移预留充足的时间，以减轻迁移带来的业务影响。

迁移前需要考虑并回答的主要问题：

1. 迁移范围（What）：本次迁移的主要内容有哪些？

在迁移之前，需要确定主要迁移的内容，例如：此次迁移是仅进行经典故事的迁移还是也有数字化董事会议厅内容的迁移；是将所有经典故事都进行迁移，还是仅迁移一些常用的故事；此次迁移是否需要一并将经典模型的迁移也纳入进来。在我们已知的客户实践中，有些客户的SAP 分析云系统中全部使用的是经典账户模型，考虑到新模型的功能优势：性能提升、增强的货币换算、更灵活的计算等（相关博客），以及新模型的战略地位和客户自身的预算限制，他们会将经典模型的迁移与经典故事的迁移打包在一起进行，以节约成本并减少业务影响。在迁移前明确迁移范围是十分必要的，因为这将对后面迁移时间和迁移参与人员的预估产生重要影响。

2. 迁移时间（When）：什么时候开始迁移？需要多长时间进行迁移？

在明确了迁移范围之后，企业需要根据实际的业务情况对迁移时间进行预估、对迁移流程进行合理的分配与安排，并留出充足的时间对迁移后的内容进行检查和试运行。

3. 参与人员（Who）：执行迁移的主要人员有哪些？迁移过程中需要哪些人员的配合？

在明确了迁移范围及时间之后，企业需要根据实际情况安排迁移的执行人员，在有条件的情况下对相关人员进行迁移的培训，并提前告知相关部门以配合迁移。

推荐的整体迁移步骤（How）：

1. 模型迁移：此步骤为非必要步骤，具体要根据企业划定的迁移范围来确定是否需要。用户可以使用建模区域中的“迁移为新模型类型”按钮将经典账户模型转换为新模型类型，该项迁移暂时不支持批量操作。以经典账户模型为例，迁移后的模型中会添加一个度量，自动设置为SignedData，用户可自行调整。模型的计算指标会自动设置，无需再进行额外操作。

模型中的“迁移为新模型类型”按钮

迁移前 - 经典账户模型的结构

迁移后 - 新模型的结构

一些需要注意的事项（相关帮助文档）：

• 迁移无法撤销。

• 如果经典账户模型已经具有相关对象（包括故事、数据操作、私有版本等）或启用了货币换算时无法进行迁移，同时为保证现有内容的可用性，请务必复制出一个模型，保证复制出的模型没有相关对象并禁用其货币换算，然后对复制的模型进行迁移。在迁移完成后再决定是否将货币换算开启，并在迁移后的模型中重新定义货币换算度量。

• 新模型类型的数据导入/导出作业与经典账户模型的旧导入/数据导入作业不兼容，因此在迁移后，必须为新模型类型创建新的数据导入/导出作业。

• 将经典账户模型迁移到新模型类型时，不会迁移数据点留言。

2. 故事迁移：请注意，优化故事体验并不包含经典故事中目前支持的所有功能，这些使用限制计划将逐渐被移除或改进。因此在进行故事迁移前，请阅读此关于优化故事体验的限制。该项迁移支持批量操作，请阅读此博客以获取更多批量迁移相关的详情。由于故事迁移具有不可逆性，同时也为了保证报告本身的访问权限不需要重新设置，请务必复制出一份故事副本作为备份，并直接在原有报告上进行迁移。

故事批量转换工具的入口

单一故事转换按钮

在经典设计体验中，故事中可能会既有网格页面，也有画布或响应式页面。由于网格页面在新的“优化设计体验”中不再支持，所以网格页面的整体迁移过程包括：

1) 用优化故事体验新建原网格页面中的报表

2) 在经典故事中删除网格页面，然后将其他页面先转换为“优化设计体验”

3) 将步骤2) 中转换后的故事和步骤1) 中的内容进行整合

3. 切换模型依赖项的模型：此步骤为非必要步骤，若进行了模型迁移则该步骤为必要步骤。此处的模型依赖项主要包括故事、数据操作等跟模型绑定的对象。在故事迁移完成之后，迁移后的故事及故事中的数据操作等内容依旧是与经典模型进行绑定的，则此时我们需要将这些对象中的经典模型替换成新模型。SAP 分析云支持在故事中将“经典账户模型”替换为为同样结构的“含账户和度量的新模型”。故事的模型替换方法及注意事项，您可以参阅此博客。数据操作的模型替换，可以直接在数据操作中进行更改，并且数据操作的模型替换允许撤销，但为保证报表的正常应用，我们仍建议备份相关的数据操作。

在故事中替换模型

在数据操作中替换模型

4. 故事的整合：此步骤为非必要步骤，需要根据实际情况判断是否需要进行。需要进行故事整合的一些用户案例：

• 经典故事中既有网格页面，又有画布或响应式页面；

• 有数字化董事会议厅内容，并且该内容由不同的经典故事组成；

• 故事的查看者期望通过一个统一的入口查看不同的故事内容；

故事的整合有两种基本的方式，一种是通过复制粘贴来实现（SAP 帮助文档），另一种是通过导入故事页面来实现（SAP 帮助文档）。通过复制粘贴，用来复制的原始故事跟粘贴后的目标故事是两个独立的内容，也就是说，在原始故事上的更改无法同步到目标故事；而通过导入故事页面，原始故事上的修改可以被SAP 分析云识别，用户可以在左侧面板中的“大纲”项下选择将内容同步。您可以根据企业的实际业务需要，在这两种方式当中进行选择。

在复杂的企业实践当中，故事的迁移是一个牵一发而动全身的项目。在阅读本文后，如果您对迁移还有疑问或者还需要我们提供哪些方面的帮助和支持，您可以在评论区进行留言。

entry.title: CDS : How to define analytical query using cell definition
entry.content:
This blog is about how to use cell definition in CDS analytical query as like in BW query. Lets start with brief overview about two structures.

 

Two Structures

The structure forms the foundational layout of the axes (rows or columns) in a data table, determining how information is organized and presented. The arrangement of the structure affects the order and quantity of key figures or characteristics displayed in the rows and columns of a query or report. More information can be found in  CDS : How to define analytical query using two str... - SAP Community

Cell Definition

A cell is the intersection between two structural components. Each cell in grid is defined by an element of the measures-structure and one of the characteristic-structure. 

Exception cell: When implicit cell definition overwritten by an explicit definition, cell is called as exception cell. It can be defined using formula or selection. Formulas for a cell must only use operands which are cells. 

Help cell: It does not have any direct connection with the corresponding structural elements. It is not embedded in the grid and not displayed, but can be used in formulas of an exception cell. 

In BW modelling tool query designer, In 'Cell' tab we can specify the cell definition.

In the above screenshot marked,

Red    : Exception cell 

Green : Formula to calculate the value for the exception cell

Blue   : Help cell

In analytical query to define the Exception cell, following annotation is used.

 

@AnalyticsDetails.query: {

  isCell: true,

  cellReference: {

    measureStructureElement: 'seatsocc',

    characteristicStructureElement: 'yearPlaceholder'

  }

}

 

A cell is specified with “@AnalyticsDetails.query.isCell: true”,

  • If it should overwrite a cell of the grid, then the coordinates can be specified with @AnalyticsDetails.query.cellReference (Exception Cell)
  • Otherwise, the element is invisible and must be hidden Consumption.hidden:true. (Help Cell)

A value of a cell in the grid can be obtained with CDS function - GET_CELL_REFERENCE_VALUE, which can be used in formula of an exception or help cell. 

Example

In the following example, 'exceptionCellRatioPercent' is Exception cell, cellReference defines exact coordinate of this cell. 'helpCellratio' is a helper cell, it does not have cellReference, it is not part of grid and has Consumption.hidden:true. It is used in formula of an exception cell 'exceptionCellRatioPercent' to calculate percentage of occupied seats.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'Query with structure and cell definition'
@ObjectModel.modelingPattern: #ANALYTICAL_QUERY
@ObjectModel.supportedCapabilities: [#ANALYTICAL_QUERY]
// CDS Query like the normal query ZLR_2STRUCT_CELL_CAL
define transient view entity ZLR_2STRUCT_CELL_REF
provider contract analytical_query
as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT
{

//Elements of the Key Figure Structure
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Max Seats'
seatsmax,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@EndUserText.label: 'Occupied Seats'
seatsocc,


//Elements of the Characteristic Structure
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: '2020'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
case when flyear = '2020' then 1 else null end as year2020,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
@EndUserText.label: 'Occupied %'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.onCharacteristicStructure: true
case when flyear = '0000' then 1 else null end as yearPlaceholder,


abap.unit'%' as unitPercent,

//Help cell - used in formula of exception cell
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@AnalyticsDetails.query: {
  isCell: true  
}
@Consumption.hidden: true
// scalar function with use of cell refernce
ratio_of( portion => GET_CELL_REFERENCE_VALUE(measure_struc_element => $projection.seatsocc, characteristic_struc_element => $projection.year2020 ),
total   => GET_CELL_REFERENCE_VALUE(measure_struc_element => $projection.seatsmax, characteristic_struc_element => $projection.year2020) )  as helpCellratio,


// Exception cell 
@Aggregation.default: #FORMULA
@AnalyticsDetails.query: {
  isCell: true,
  cellReference: {
    measureStructureElement: 'seatsocc',
    characteristicStructureElement: 'yearPlaceholder'
  
  }
}
@AnalyticsDetails.query.decimals: 2
@Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure: 'unitPercent'
$projection.helpCellratio * 100 as exceptionCellRatioPercent

 
}

 

Output

More information can be found Cell, Exception Cell and Help Cell | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Creating 'Reset' button in SAC Stories
entry.content:
When working with the SAC reports, it is obvious ask from the users that they want to have a Reset button which could help them to instantly come back to the landing page of the story. At the moment, SAC doesn't have any such standard buttons/functionality available which user can bring in to the report and use for resetting all the values/filters.

 

However, with the use of scripting method we can achieve this! I'm going to share a glimpse of how to write a short script to get the reset button.

 

With the above screenshot, you can see that the 'Reset' button is placed next to other filters. The purpose of the button is to bring back all the filters to 'All' value or to the value which user wish to have and avoid manual resetting.

 

Code used for the Reset Button:

InputControl_1.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_2.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_3.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_4.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_5.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_6.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_7.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_8.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_9.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

InputControl_13.getInputControlDataSource().setAllMembersSelected();

Another observation from the provided screenshot if you make is that we do have Monthly Detailed/Yearly Detailed and Hide Button. This Reset method is applied to these radio button group code as well where the tabular data is getting reset as well.

if(RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey()==="Value 1")
{
Monthly_TabStrip.setVisible(true);

Table_1.setVisible(true);

InputControl_10.setVisible(true);

Chart_20.setVisible(true);

InputControl_11.setVisible(true);

YTD_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_2.setVisible(false);

InputControl_12.setVisible(false);

Chart_21.setVisible(false);

InputControl_14.setVisible(false);
}
else if(RadioButtonGroup_1.getSelectedKey()==="Value 2")
{
Monthly_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_1.setVisible(false);

InputControl_10.setVisible(false);

Chart_20.setVisible(false);

InputControl_11.setVisible(false);

YTD_TabStrip.setVisible(true);

Table_2.setVisible(true);

InputControl_12.setVisible(true);

Chart_21.setVisible(true);

InputControl_14.setVisible(true);
}
else
{

Monthly_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_1.setVisible(false);

InputControl_10.setVisible(false);

Chart_20.setVisible(false);

InputControl_11.setVisible(false);

YTD_TabStrip.setVisible(false);

Table_2.setVisible(false);

InputControl_12.setVisible(false);

Chart_21.setVisible(false);

InputControl_14.setVisible(false);
}

 

Happy Learning!

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud Using Data Import API
entry.content:
This blog post aims to bring some introduction about the usage of SAC Data Import APIs and how they can be leveraged to replicate data to Public Dimensions. It will also describe of one use case implemented recently to fulfill particular requirements without going with the standard communication scenario.

Data Import APIs can be leveraged to replicate transactional and master data to SAP Analytics Cloud.

The API includes 5 different services that can be used via API endpoints. These can be tested using an API Client or Platform such as Postman, Insomnia.

  • Models service
  • PublicDimensions service
  • CurrencyConversions service
  • Jobs service
  • Import service

For more information about the these 5 services please consult this document inHelp Portal.

Also available inSAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Use Case (Example): Replicate Enterprise Project individually to SAP Analytics Cloud after creation or change in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Purpose of Use Case:

Replicate Enterprise Projects based on creation (or change) action in S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Keep in mind this is just an example originated from a real requirement but can be adapted, with the respective complexity, to any other object if it fulfills the necessary conditions and systems involved to satisfy the need to replicate an object on individual basis from S4 Public Cloud to SAC.

Ultimately, this is available for any other middleware or point-to-point consumption if the Sender system allows it.

An High-Level Diagram with the components involved is available below:

  • One SAP BTP Subaccount where Event Mesh and Integration Suite are subscribed with respective instances and applications. For more information how to setup the Event Mesh from SAP Integration Suite check the following document.
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud
  • SAP Analytics Cloud
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services is a nice-to-have in this example but logically for productive scenarios it shall be included in the overall architecture.

 

Image Caption: Technical Architecture Design

 

 

After understanding which components are used in this scenario, next level Diagram explains the data flow between each SAP component to leverage the Data Import API. Remember that it can be adapted to use one of the other 5 services although might require adjustments, especially in the interface in SAP Integration Suite.

Pre-Requisites:

  • SAP Event Mesh and SAP Integration Suite integrated
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud Integrated with SAP Integration Suite, using for example OAUTH Client Credentials
  • S/4HANA Public Cloud Integrated with SAP Event Mesh. Consult the following document in case more information is needed to perform this step.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud configured with one Public Dimension and with OAUTH Authentication configured to be consumed by SAP Integration Suite. For more information how to configure the authentication, consult this document.

 

Image Caption: Process Flow Architecture Diagram

 

 

As legend for the above diagram, explaining the data flow process:

Business User executes a create or change action on the respective business object. In this use case the Enterprise Project.

  1. Create or Change event in S/4HANA Public Cloud releases that outbound even message towards Event Mesh, previously configured;
  2. Event Mesh consumes and subsequently is consumed by SAP Integration Suite;
  3. Once the event is received, SAP Integration Suite extracts the Enterprise Project ID to then execute a GET Call based on the Enterprise Project ODATA API to receive the object data payload to deliver to SAP Analytics Cloud;
  4. SAP Integration Suite interface needs to transform the payload into the structure the Public Dimension expects to successfully replicate it. This has been done via message mapping element but can be accomplished with a script as well;
  5. Finally, the delivery of the message to the dataimport API requires first an authentication, using OAUTH mechanism for example to post the data into the respective PublicDimension which will be defined in the HTTP endpoint.
    1. At this final step, as described in the documentation, SAC demands the CSRF Token which needs to be retrieved first and sent as Header in the final step. This step is described in this document.

 

The following diagram presents a simple example of an interface executing all the steps mentioned above from SAP Integration Suite perspective.

 

Image Caption: The main integration flow with all the steps to accomplish a simple scenario without complex error handling mechanism.

 

Image Caption: Local Integration Process to Post the Payload into SAP Analytics Cloud.

One important caveat to be successful is to understand the Public Dimension data structure. All the necessary attributes, either mandatory or optional, need to be included in the message mapping. If not, the execution will fail.

This can be either assessed from SAC UI, going into the Public Dimension and check the properties.

Image Caption: SAP Analytics Cloud – Modeler – Public Dimension

 

Alternatively, via GET Call, using for example Postman, this information can also be retrieved using the Public Dimension metadata, using the following URL:

https://{host}/api/v1/dataimport/publicDimensions/{publicDimensionID}/metadata

This procedure is explained in this document. One might see that in the Sample Response, all attributes part of the Public Dimension are retrieved with their respective data types and key identification.

Image Caption: Public Dimension Metadata Response Sample

 

 In case of failed executions/messages, SAC also provides a mechanism to verify and understand the job failure. If you remember, I mentioned the dataimport API includes 5 services, being one of them for “Jobs”.

For example /jobs/{jobID}/invalidRows can be used to assess what failed during the data import into the respective Public Dimension.

There are additional examples available in SAP Business Accelerator HUB, such as S/4HANA Pricing Information to SAP Analytics Cloud.

Hope this particular scenario can be of any service for particular situations and requirements.

Happy testing and continuous learning. Share with us any interesting use cases you have implemented which can support others and reach out in case of any issues.

SAP Analytics Cloud #SAC Data Import API SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Future Proofing Your Data Strategy: Unlocking the Power of AI to Drive Business Innovation - Part 1
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, data has become a strategic asset and the lifeblood of modern enterprises. The ability to harness and leverage data effectively can mean the difference between thriving and merely surviving. With the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence (AI), the importance of a robust data strategy has never been greater.

Organizations wanting to adopt AI must treat data investment as a strategic initiative. This involves creating a data strategy that aligns with business goals while leveraging the latest technologies to unlock the full potential of their data assets. When managing organization’s data across disparate data solutions, the data teams struggle with maintaining the business context, data quality and trust in the data. In an SAP survey of 1200 IT and Business leaders in the second half of 2024, only 34% business leaders report high trust in their data capabilities and 55% state that Data Quality remains their Number 1 challenge. (see figure 1)

Trust in data doesn’t happen by accident—it’s built through strong governance frameworks that help ensure data is accurate, consistent, and actionable. Without governance, organizations risk poor decisions, fragmented insights, and a lack of confidence in data systems. Our survey and interviews highlight governance as a critical factor in closing the trust gap and enabling data-oriented success.

 

Figure 1 - SAP Survey Results - 2H 2024

Recognizing this need, SAP introduced the SAP Business Data Cloud, a solution offering designed to unify all SAP and third-party data within an organization in once place for both Analytics and AI use cases. It provides a trusted data foundation and the tools necessary to make impactful decisions while fostering reliable AI. By integrating SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Databricks and SAP Business Warehouse into a unified experience, SAP Business Data Cloud delivers actionable insights across all lines of business.

The Need for a Future Ready Data Strategy in the Age of AI 

The demand for AI and advanced analytics has underscored the critical need for a future ready AI first data strategy. AI’s growing influence is evident in the implementation of AI agents—intelligent systems that learn, make decisions, and execute actions on behalf of humans. McKinsey & Company refers to this concept as “superagency,” describing it as potentially transformative as the steam engine during the Industrial Revolution.

According to McKinsey’s January 2025 report, superagency represents the enhanced capabilities achieved through human-AI collaboration. By leveraging AI tools, individuals can amplify creativity, productivity, and decision-making, creating a partnership that elevates human and machine potential. However, this transformation heavily relies on accessible, integrated, and high-quality data.

Traditional data strategies, often characterized by multiple data solutions pieced together, creating silos and limiting AI’s ability to function across the business context. Legacy data systems may also struggle to handle the volume and velocity required by AI. Without a Future Ready data strategy, AI tools risk becoming costly, underperforming investments, unable to deliver the return on investment (ROI) they promise. A Future Ready data strategy bridges the gap between AI’s potential and an organization’s readiness, transforming raw data into a trustworthy, scalable asset that empowers leaders and employees alike.

Advantages of a Robust Future Data Strategy 

In addition to ensuring accurate, reliable, and trustworthy data outcomes essential for successful AI implementation, a Future Ready data strategy offers numerous advantages that can help and accelerate the transformation of an organization by focusing on:

  • Access to trusted accurate and timely data insights enables better-informed decisions. 
  • Streamlining processes to enhance operational efficiency across different data roles
  • Minimizes data redundancy lowering the risk of data breaches leading to significant cost savings.
  • Reveals market trends and customer needs that can foster a culture of continuous innovation.
  • Avoids legal issues and penalties through adherence to data protection regulations
  • protects sensitive information, building trust with robust security measures.

Current Trends in Data Architecture Frameworks 

As data continues to grow in both volume and complexity, organizations must adopt modern data architecture frameworks to address these challenges effectively.

The Trends include:

  • Data Fabric: A unified architecture that ensures consistent data management across various storage types. 
  • Data Mesh: A decentralized approach that empowers individual business domains to own and manage their data as products. 
  • Data Products: Tools or solutions designed to provide end users with actionable insights for decision-making.

SAP Business Data Cloud: The Solution 

SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) addresses the challenges of legacy data architectures, enabling organizations to unlock the full potential of their data assets (See figure 2). As a fully managed SaaS solution by SAP, it unifies and governs all SAP data while seamlessly integrating with third-party data sources. This empowers business leaders to make impactful decisions with contextual insights.

Key features of SAP Business Data Cloud include: 

  • Mission-Critical Data Products - Access to all SAP data across critical business processes in a deeply contextual manner. 
  • Seamless Integration - Native capabilities to connect SAP and third-party data, including integration with Databricks. 
  • Insight Apps - Prebuilt applications that deliver specific insights for advanced analytics and planning across all business lines. 
  • Trusted Data Foundation - A reliable foundation for production-ready AI that ensures data is accurate, responsible, and relevant. 

By providing tools for creating AI models and generative AI applications trained on business data, SAP Business Data Cloud fosters reliable AI. Its trusted data layer ensures AI agents can access all organizational data, while the SAP Knowledge Graph connects data, metadata, and business processes. This contextual connection enables AI agents to ground their decisions in the original business context, ensuring accuracy and relevance.

Figure 2 - SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC)

Modernizing SAP Business Warehouse with SAP Business Data Cloud 

For organizations currently using SAP Business Warehouse (BW), SAP Business Data Cloud offers a clear path forward. It provides an economical route to cloud modernization while preserving existing BW capabilities. By leveraging Business Data Cloud, organizations can maximize their BW data as data products, take advantage of advanced AI capabilities, and modernize their infrastructure. Native integration with SAP BW simplifies the transition to the cloud, ensuring customers can maximize their SAP investments.

Conclusion 

According to McKinsey, only 1% of leaders consider their organizations to be AI-mature. Barriers such as talent shortages, ethical considerations, infrastructure challenges, and fragmented data limit AI adoption. Among these, data quality and integration stand out as critical success factors.

Fragmented, low-quality data trapped in silos undermines AI’s ability to deliver actionable insights and closed-loop actions. A robust data strategy, supported by solutions like SAP Business Data Cloud, can overcome these challenges. It not only lays the foundation for future AI applications but also delivers immediate value by providing insights that surprise and delight business users.

By making their data strategies future ready, organizations can unlock the full potential of AI, driving innovation and achieving sustainable growth.

A big shout out to the Co-Authors: @jasonluo @ManjunathGudisi. Thank you!

 

SAP Business Data Cloud  SAP Analytics Cloud BW (SAP Business Warehouse)

entry.title: 利用自定义微件在SAP 分析云中创建列表型的报表
entry.content:
在当今数据驱动的时代，数据可视化工具以直观的图表、动态的交互和实时的数据更新成为了人们的焦点，但传统业务报表却并未因此退出舞台。传统业务报表因为用户习惯、合规要求、特定场景需求（如：打印等）依旧在企业中扮演着重要角色，然而在SAP 分析云的客户实践中，传统的业务报表需求通常难以被很好地满足。在本篇博客中，我将分享一个由 SAP 分析云开发爱好者 Linna Zhang（GitHub主页）开发的自定义微件（下载地址：中文版 | 英文版），并结合一些常见的用户案例来帮助您了解并利用该自定义微件。

目前该自定义微件已经实现的一些功能：

  • 插入新行/列
  • 支持单元格公式计算（如：SUM, MAX, MIN）
  • 合并单元格
  • 缩进设置
  • 对齐方式设置
  • 单元格重命名
  • 自定义单位、数据格式、小数点位数
  • 设置为百分数展示、取消百分数展示
  • 拖动以调整列宽
  • 隐藏表头

自定义表格微件 - 单元格上下文菜单

自定义表格微件 – 附加属性设置

通过以上功能，我们能在SAP 分析云中创建列表型的报表。以下是一些示例：

分组报表

 

行式报表

 

复合式报表

 

使用该自定义微件时，需要注意以下事项：

  • 表格里任何的内容更改都不会改动模型，这些更改仅仅只存在该自定义微件的属性里；
  • 自定义表格微件一旦识别到绑定的数据结构有更改时，该微件上所有的设置都会被重置。因此，在设计表格时，请务必确保绑定的数据结构不再变动后（即所有生成器面板中的设置不再更改后），再进行报表上的调整和样式设置；
  • 表格中的公式计算不支持通过点击/框选直接引用单元格及区域，必须手动输入单元格位置或区域。因此该微件并不适用于颗粒度小、数据加工量多的报表。

自定义表格微件在一定程度上能满足SAP 分析云客户的传统报表需求，如果要被更广泛地应用，还可以进一步优化以下功能：

  • 字体、单元格样式设置
  • 自定义条件格式
  • 表格中的公式计算通过点击/框选直接引用单元格及区域
  • 性能及稳定性

除此以外，自定义表格微件中还有哪些功能你认为可以进一步优化的呢？欢迎您在评论区留言讨论。希望今天分享的自定义表格微件能够为各位开发者提供一些思路，帮助各位开发出更多满足需求并且功能强大的自定义微件。

 

 

*声明：该微件仅出于学习和沟通目的进行分享，不适用于生产。在使用之前，请务必检查并彻底阅读其中使用的任何第三方库的许可证。使用此资源库中的示例，即表示您确认您已了解并同意遵守这些许可中规定的所有条款和条件。请负责任地使用此资源包，使用此资源包产生的任何风险将由您自行承担。

entry.title: 入门教程如何将 SAP Digital Boardroom 数字化董事会资产迁移到演示模式（上）
entry.content:
为何使用演示模式？ 

为了简化 SAP Analytics Cloud 资产架构并为我们的客户提供提高效率的工具，我们将 SAP Digital Boardroom 的一些核心功能直接集成到了优化故事体验中。这一改进适用于所有客户。 

根据现有SAP Digital Boardroom客户的反馈，我们希望确保演示模式能够解决一些关键痛点，包括性能问题以及功能一致性。 

有关演示模式的更多信息，请阅读博客：演示模式：在优化体验中集成核心 Digital Boardroom 功能 

本教程将帮助大家深入了解如何将现有的 Digital Boardroom 替换为演示模式。这一过程包括两个步骤： 

  1. 将经典资产（故事或分析应用）迁移到优化故事体验
  2. 利用优化体验中的新功能（例如页面导入和演示配置）构建演示模式下的Digital Boardroom体验 

 

将经典资产（故事或分析应用）迁移到优化故事体验 

演示模式是优化故事体验的扩展功能，这意味着在使用演示模式之前，需要将相关资产迁移到优化故事体验中。       

  • 您可以参考现有的帮助文档，了解如何迁移现有资产的详细信息。 
  • 您可以点击此处查看优化体验的当前限制信息。 
  • 您还可以点击此处查看SAP分析云经典模型和故事迁移的详细信息。 

需要注意的是，从经典故事体验转换为优化故事体验时，会提供两种选项： 

  • 转换：它会将您的经典资产转换为使用优化设计体验。您将能够继续使用相同的故事ID。 

注意：这意味着现有的Digital Boardroom在使用该资产时会处于损坏状态，因为SAP Analytics Cloud 不支持在优化故事体验中使用资产。 

  • 转换+另存为：它会克隆您的经典资产，并将克隆的版本转换为优化设计体验。您的原始故事保留原有的故事ID ，而克隆的版本将拥有一个新的故事ID。 

注意：这意味着原本可以访问该故事的用户将无法访问转换后的版本，且与该故事关联的现有书签也将不可再用。建议将此选项作为迁移原始资产之前的验证步骤使用。

您必须对SAP Digital Boardroom 仪表盘中使用的所有故事重复此过程。然后，我们将打开其中一个故事（建议选择原始SAP Digital Boardroom中页面数量最多的故事），并通过导入其他已转换故事的所有页面或部分页面来构建演示结构。 

 

让我们来看一个示例： 

我们有一个经典SAP Digital Boardroom资产，它使用了一个故事，其结构如下图所示： 

 

在此 SAP Digital Boardroom 资产中使用的故事： 

我们将在这里演示一个故事的迁移过程，我们选择迁移“Demo_15429_BestRunBikes”，因为它较为复杂。 

我们打开故事，并在“保存”菜单中选择“转换为优化设计体验”。 

 系统会首先列出阻碍迁移的功能特性。 

在对阻碍迁移的表格进行了必要的修改之后，我们保存了故事，并选择了“转换为优化设计体验”。 

由于所有阻碍迁移的功能特性都已处理，我们获得了两种选择：原地迁移（in-place migration）或将迁移后的资产保存为一个新故事。我们已经在上面分享了每种方法的优点。在这里，我们选择“转换 + 另存为”，这将帮助我们使用经典SAP Digital Boardroom资产来验证演示结构。 

 

构建演示结构 

导入故事页面 

在“Demo_15429_BestRunBike 优化设计体验”的编辑模式中，我们现在提供了一个新的添加页面工作流选项，称为“导入故事页面”。您也可以通过页面标签栏中的“添加页面”按钮，或者大纲面板中页面部分的“+”号来访问此功能。 

在这里，我们将导入“Demo_15429_Landing_Page 优化设计体验”（已经迁移好的第二个故事）。 

统一故事还允许用户导入隐藏页面，这些页面在目标故事中也会保持隐藏状态。 

此处的“概览”页面在原始故事中是隐藏的。 

导入完成后，SAP Analytics Cloud（SAC）会提供状态更新。 

完成上述流程后，保存故事。 

 

演示配置 

配置演示的选项可以通过大纲面板中的“演示”选项访问，也可以通过编辑故事工具栏（扳手图标）下的“演示设置”访问。 

在演示设置中，设计者可以灵活地将页面归类到“主题”或“子主题”下。“主题”和“子主题”的组合将形成一个树状结构，以指导演示者进行演示。在构建演示时，需要注意以下几点： 

  • 配置演示结构并不是强制性的。我们将“不分层显示”作为演示模式的默认选项。 
  • 演示结构没有根节点，并为设计者提供了一个简单的单通道配置选项，可以通过拖放功能重新排列。 
  • 在任何时候，都可以通过删除所有主题和子主题来恢复为“不分层显示”（此功能在受控版本中不可以使用）。 
  • 在演示结构中，并非必须使用原始故事中的所有页面。未使用的页面将在演示模式下保持隐藏状态。 
  • 我们限制每个页面在演示结构中只能被使用一次。 
  • 您可以选择隐藏“演示结构”中的主题，这将导致该主题下的所有子主题和页面也会被隐藏。此外，在“查看”模式下隐藏的页面在“演示”模式下也会保持隐藏状态。 

  • 我们还提供了一个选项，可以在故事中显示隐藏页面。在故事中隐藏的页面在演示模式下也会保持隐藏状态。此外，如果某个主题或子主题下只有这一个隐藏页面，那么该主题或子主题也会保持隐藏状态。 

  • 在一个演示结构中，每个页面只能被使用一次。 

完成演示结构的配置后，保存故事并点击演示模式（位于右上角）。 

注意：用户可以随时在查看模式和演示模式之间切换显示故事。 

最终效果如下所示： 

 

自定义工具栏位置 

这里的工具栏是浮动的。我们将其设置到屏幕底部，以模仿Digital Boardroom中的操作栏。为此，请导航至“故事设置”（扳手图标）中的“查看时设置”下的“工具栏”。 

选择模式为“演示”。工具栏的默认配置是“浮动”，我们将把它改为“固定”，并选择屏幕位置为“底部”。 

 

“自动显示和隐藏工具栏”功能仅在用户将光标移动到屏幕顶部或底部时才会显示工具栏，具体取决于工具栏的屏幕位置设置。 

 

SAP Analytics Cloud 资源 

关于故事与分析应用程序的统一，更多信息请参阅Jie Deng的博客文章。 

关于演示模式是什么以及如何进一步自定义，更多信息请参阅Tunir Kapil的博客文章。 

 

 

原文链接：How to migrate SAP Digital Boardroom Dashboard to ... - SAP Community  

原作者：Vivek-Varma

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 6: SAP Analytics Cloud & SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
This sixth post in my series gives an overview of latest user experience (UX) innovations in SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The highlights for SAP Analytics Cloud are the support for the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, giving users an experience consistent with other products; the Compass for business users to easily do Monte-Carlo simulations; Video Data Stories, and enhancements to the just ask natural language query capability. For SAP BTP, have a look at the new UX for the SAP Integration Suite, SAP BTP Kyma runtime, and SAP Build Apps.

 

SAP Analytics Cloud

In addition to full support for the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, the Q1/2025 release of SAP Analytics Cloud released on February 26th brings some great new experiences for users. I’ll focus here on these three:

  • The new Compass for easy Monte Carlo simulations by business users.
  • Video Data Stories, enabling business users to easily create videos to present their stories.
  • Just Ask enhancements.

Subscribe to the SAP D&A Quarterly Newsletter to get the latest news, customer stories, events, and innovations delivered directly to your inbox.

Support for SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon

SAP Analytics Cloud now also supports the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon – not only for embedded uses cases as before, but now stand-alone too. As a result, users of our cloud applications can have a consistent experience when switching to SAP Analytics Cloud. Figure 1 shows an example.

Figure 1: SAP Analytics Cloud with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: An example screenshot showing what chart, table, input control and widgets look like with the Horizon visual theme.

Find out more in this blog post:

  • Horizon Theme and Templates for Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud.

SAP Analytics Cloud Compass for Monte Carlo Simulation

Non-technical users can now easily perform real-time multivariant analysis without prior IT setup or advanced statistic skills. You can trigger a new compass simulation via the toolbar or directly from a dashboard. If things are not going as planned, for example the gross revenue in the current quarter, you can trigger a simulation which will automatically detect the drivers and display probability distributions for gross revenue.

You can choose between low, medium or high precision, which uses 1,000, 10,000 or 100,000 iterations respectively. As you can see in Figure 2, this is visualized interactively, showing the probability for the current value. This is visualized by showing how likely the operating income will be exceeded (23% in the example) and how likely it will not be achieved (77%). At the bottom, you can see ranges for the pessimistic, realistic and optimistic cases (lower 5%, middle 90% and upper 5%). Since it is interactive, you can change the planned value and directly see how likely you are to exceed or not achieve it – as you can see in Figure 2.

Figure 2: SAP Analytics Cloud Compass for Monte Carlo Simulation by business users. ALT Text: This short embedded GIF video shows a screen with a header “Target: Operating Income / Amount GC – Million USD”. Below that, a chart showing a probability distribution for Operating Income, looking similar to a normal distribution. It has a vertical line at 87.51, indicating that the probability of less income is 5% (labelled “Pessimistic Case”, and a vertical line at 175.20, indicating the probability of more income is 5% (labelled “Optimistic Case”). The 90% band in between is labelled “Realistic Case”, with a slider at 151.67 (shown in a box above the slider), with 77% shown on the left and 23% on the right (the probability of income being lower or higher). The user moves the slider to the left with the mouse to the value 106.10, and the two values to the left and right change to 16% and 84%. Then the user types in a value of 100, resulting in 12% and 88%.

Find out more about this, and watch it in action in this video:

  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025 (Section 2 on Compass) (2:35 min.)

Video Data Stories

Another great user experience innovation with the latest release is the ability for business users to directly create short videos to present their data stories out of SAP Analytics Cloud – Figure 3 shows an example.

Figure 3: Part of a Video Data Story created from SAP Analytics Cloud. ALT Text: A short video with animated text and charts in a mobile phone vertical format. It starts with the title “Sep 2024, results”, followed by “Gross Margin”. Then the text “$8.66 million” glides in, followed by “delta 2024 Aug +37.45%”. Next, these disappear and “Gross Margin per Location” appears, above a horizontal bar chart with three bars for California ($6.78 M), Nevada ($0,52 M) and Oregon ($1.36 M). This chart then morphs into a new horizontal bar chart with five bars for Los Angeles ($2.45 M), Oakland ($1.22 M), San Diego ($1.62 M), San Francisco ($0.29 M), and San Jose ($1.21 M).

It is really easy to create video data stories: you can select which data you want to show, how you want to display it (Figure 4), and select a background image and background music. We provide a selection of images and music out of the box for you to choose from.

Figure 4: Creating Video Data Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud. ALT Text: The image shows part of a screenshot showing an SAP Analytics Cloud page with bar charts and a pie chart, partly covered by a section below which shows five views of the video data story, containing (from the left) the title, a horizontal bar chart with three bars, a pie chart, a line chart, and a KPI as a number.

Find out more in this blog post, these videos and the documentation:

  • Video Data Story, a brand-new way of story telling in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Video: Video Data Story – Sample Analysis Report (33 seconds).
  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025 (Section 3 on Video Data Stories) (1:30 min.)
  • What’s New Viewer – SAP Analytics Cloud.

Just Ask Enhancements

The popular just ask feature enables you to use natural language to query SAP Analytics Cloud. It was introduced with this blog post:

  • Initial Release of SAP Analytics Cloud Just Ask.

With the Q1/2025 release, we improve just ask by supporting currency conversion in acquired data models. We also allow users to limit the number of data points in bar charts, as well as leveraging SAP HANA Cloud vector engine.

Currency conversion means that, if you have data with multiple currencies, you can change the currency used to summarize all the values using natural language. Also, you can choose to show all the results in the respective local currency, so that for example the mouse-over shows the details and the currency for that country, as shown in Figure 5, where the user has asked “sales in local currency actual compared to forecast”.

Figure 5: SAP Analytics Cloud just ask now supports currency conversion – here you see a visualization in local currency per country. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot with Just Ask at the top left, with a text entry field containing “sales in local currency actual compared to forecast”. Below that, a horizontal bar chart showing “Top 10 Sales in Local Currency per Country”, with countries Denmark, Belgium, Italy, Sweden, France etc. Wherever the actual is higher than forecast the difference is marked with a green bar, where it is lower with a red bar. The user has placed the mouse over the green bar for Sweden, showing a popover containing the text “Sales in Local Currency, SEK909.2 k, Country Sweden, in green: Actual – Forecast SEK168.7K”.

Watch it in action here:

  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025 (Section 4 on just ask) (1:34 min.)

Further Innovations and Outlook on Joule Analytical Pattern

You can see the above three innovations, plus “Seamless Planning” and “Job Monitor – Multi-Action support” in this video:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features in Q1 2025.

As I mentioned in Part 1 of this series, we plan to make the new analytical pattern for Joule available in Q2/2025, which will be powered by this latest release of SAP Analytics Cloud (as usual for forward-looking statements, our plans are subject to change). This means users of products that support Joule, such as SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP SuccessFactors, will be able to use Joule to query SAP Analytics Cloud and get analytical insights directly within Joule (the Joule Analytical Pattern). It will be powered by SAP Analytics Cloud, with data from models that are indexed by the Just Ask feature of SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

SAP Business Technology Platform

SAP Integration Suite

The SAP Integration Suite now also supports the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, as you can see below in Figure 6.

Figure 6: SAP Integration Suite with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: The image shows three overlapping screenshots: at the left, back, “Employee Service” with buttons for triggering GET, POST, GET, PATCH URLs. Next, in the middle, the Capabilities page showing three rows of four columns of cards such as “Build Integration Scenarios”, “Manage APIs”, “Manage Business Events”, each with two to three lines description. On the right a page showing details of “SAP S/4HANA Cloud Integration with Workday”. The “Artifacts” tab has been selected, showing a list of artifacts with columns Name, Type, Version and Actions. The first entry has a name “Integrate Workday employee information”, the second one “Replicate Cost Centers from SAP S/4HANA Cloud to Workday”.

SAP BTP Cockpit

Customers can now get an easy to understand overview of their costs and usage of SAP Business Technology Platform with the new costs and usage application (Figure 7).

Figure 7: SAP BTP Cockpit – Costs and Usage. ALT Text: The image shows an example screenshot. The header reads “Global Account: Automotive Rentals, Inc. – Costs and Usage”, below that a small card “Global Account Info” with Account ID, Account Type “Customer”, Contract Currency “USD” and a wide card “Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement” with a chart showing Cloud Credit Usage, a KPI value for the Monthly Cloud Credit Usage and a vertical bar chart for Monthly Cloud Credit Usage Trend. Below that the majority of the screen shows the Billing tab, with the text “This view displays the monthly billable service charges for your global account”. Below that a flexible column layout with a list of Service Plans on the left, with “Business Application Studio – standard-edition” selected, and on the right a hierarchical table showing Directories and Subaccounts. At the bottom a filled line chart showing the cumulative list prices per month over the last 12 months.

SAP BTP, Kyma Runtime

Kyma now supports modules, for adding functionalities to your cluster. To manage these, the Kyma dashboard has been redesigned, with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, as you can see in Figure 8.

Figure 8: Kyma dashboard with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon, now supporting modules. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot with the “Cluster Overview” selected in the left side navigation bar. The screen has a large header card “Introducing Modules” with buttons for “Add Modules” and “Learn More”. Below that a section “Resource Details” containing one card, followed by a section “Monitoring and Health” containing further cards (“Memory Usage”, “CPU Usage”). A further smaller screenshot covers the bottom right of the other one, showing a Modules column in the middle, and a wider “Add Modules” panel on the right, containing four cards “SAP BTP Operator”, “Keda”, “Serverless”, “Telemetry”.

SAP Build Apps

SAP Build Apps enable business users to define their own simple applications without having to go to their IT teams to find developers. For these users, an intuitive user experience is paramount, and hence we continuously look to see where we can make it even better. One aspect of intuitiveness is providing a consistent experience across various products – hence SAP Build Apps also runs with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon.

Figure 9: SAP Build Apps with the SAP Fiori visual theme Horizon. ALT Text: The image shows a screenshot with a heading “Choose Configuration to Build” above six square cards “Android (AAB)”, “Android (APK)”, “iOS (Ad hoc)”, “Web (MTAR)”, “Web (ZIP)”, “workzone build”. Below that a table “Build History” showing two lines for “iOS (Ad hoc)”.

Here are some of the recent improvements:

  • You can create a project version directly now by releasing the project
  • Discover, integrate, and use any preconfigured action that is published in the SAP Build Library: you can now easily discover published Actions in SAP Build Apps via the SAP Build Library and install them into your Build Apps project, consume and trigger them as part of your application’s business logic via the Trigger Action flow function.
  • In the History tab, you can now see all previously saved states of the app. Using the Restore button, you can restore the application to the respective saved state.
  • You can duplicate pages in the page preview header.
  • You can use the updated Build service, which comes with a better look and feel, and an improved user experience:
    • Build and Deploy Web Applications.
  • The details matter with UX! To improve the user experience, we have updated the navigation menu in the global toolbar (Figure 10). App developers now have access to the Variables directly from the menu. The previous UI Canvas and Data tabs are now renamed respectively to User Interface and Integrations. To save space, the less frequently used Theme, Navigation, Authentication and Help options are now grouped under the App Settings drop-down menu. In the upper right corner, you can access the Preview button. Next to it is the Publish button, which enables you either to Create a Release Version or to Build and Deploy.

Figure 10: The new navigation menu in the SAP Build Apps global toolbar. ALT Text: A screenshot showing the header of SAP Build Apps, which contains tabs “User Interface”, “Variables”, “Integrations”, and “App Settings” with a drop-down menu with entries “Navigation”, “Authentication”, “Theme”, and “Help”.

Continued soon…

I hope you enjoyed this sixth part, covering SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Business Technology Platform.

I will post the final part seven next week, covering latest innovations for UI developers, for both web and mobile.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 1: Many New Innovations Available (AI, Joule and More).

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori    
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: New Road Maps for SAP Business Network - Supplier Portal Private Edition and Commerce Automation
entry.content:
As part of our ongoing enhancements to SAP Activate Methodology content, we are excited to announce the release of two new road maps: SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Private Edition and SAP Business Network for Commerce Automation. Both road maps are now available in SAP Road Map Viewer and SAP Cloud ALM.

SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Private Edition is a cloud-based solution designed to support seamless collaboration between buyers and suppliers through a unified portal. It enables smooth data exchange with SAP S/4HANA, automates key procurement workflows, and helps drive efficiency by minimizing manual effort. The solution also enhances collaboration across the network, accelerating digital transformation and delivering better business outcomes.

This solution is now included as an entitlement for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition customers who have selected the Premium or Premium Plus service packages. These customers receive access to the core capabilities of Supplier Portal Private Edition at no additional licensing cost. Please note that project implementation, custom extensions, and optional supplier enablement services may involve additional fees.

The implementation road map follows the 5 phases of SAP Activate (Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy, and Run) and focuses on two key workstreams: Supplier enablement and Data configuration and integration.

Each phase covers key activities such as:

SAP Business Network for Commerce Automation is a cloud-based solution designed to automate procurement and invoicing processes between buyers and suppliers. It enables the efficient electronic exchange of transactional documents such as purchase orders, order confirmations, advanced ship notices, service entry sheets, and invoices, reducing manual effort and improving process efficiency.

By providing real-time visibility into transaction status and history, Commerce Automation enhances collaboration between trading partners and helps enforce procurement policies and regulatory compliance.

While Commerce Automation itself focuses on transactional automation, the data it manages can be leveraged for spend analysis and supplier performance monitoring when combined with complementary analytics solutions, such as SAP Analytics Cloud or SAP Ariba Supplier Management. These tools provide deeper insights into spend patterns, supplier performance scorecards, risk profiles, and sustainability metrics.

The Commerce Automation road map is structured into the 5 phases of SAP Activate (Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy, Run) and supports rapid, scalable deployment with AI-driven insights and faster time-to-value. The key activities across these phases include:

For a detailed view of all activities and deliverables in each phase, we encourage you to explore the SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Private Edition and SAP Business Network for Commerce Automation road maps directly in SAP Road Map Viewer or SAP Cloud ALM.

We also welcome your feedback and questions — feel free to share your thoughts directly in the comments section of this blog post. Your input helps us continuously improve the guidance and materials we provide.

Get to know more at:

New SAP Activate Implementation Roadmap for SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Public Edition

Optimize SAP Implementation with SAP Activate Methodology and Guided Implementation in Cloud ALM

SAP Business Network Commerce Automation | Procurement Collaboration

Deploying SAP Business Network Supplier Portal with SAP S/4 HANA Cloud

Unlocking Success with SAP Activate Methodology and SAP Roadmap Viewer Tool

Last 10 SAP Activate 1 Minute Video's - SAP Community

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Community

SAP Activate Community

SAP Activate Minute Playlist

entry.title: Three Reasons to Read the SAP Business Data Cloud E-Book
entry.content:
Last month, SAP announced SAP Business Data Cloud, a fully managed software-as-a-service (SaaS) solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data.   

As an evolution of our industry-leading data, planning, and analytics solutions, SAP Business Data Cloud brings together SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Business Warehouse with a unified experience that delivers transformational insights across all lines of business.   

SAP also announced a landmark partnership with Databricks that brings the power of Databricks directly into SAP Business Data Cloud. This marks a new era in enterprise data management, with two leaders in their domains coming together to redefine how applications and data platforms work together. 

Our new e-book, Unleash Transformative Insights with SAP Business Data Cloud, offers valuable insights into the product, including more information about the core features, architecture, and use cases.  

Here's why you should check out this e-book: 

1. Deep Dive into Core Features 

Explore the inner workings of SAP Business Data Cloud and discover how it can fuel every part of your organization with harmonized data. This e-book provides an overview of its core functionalities, from insight apps to data products. By mastering these features, you'll be equipped to drive your business forward with data-informed decisions. 

2. Visualize the Architecture 

The e-book includes an architecture diagram of SAP Business Data Cloud, illustrating how the various components work together to deliver a unified data foundation. This visual guide will help you comprehend the architecture's complexity and appreciate how it unifies SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Business Warehouse, and SAP Databricks into a cohesive ecosystem. 

3. Explore Real-World Use Cases 

One of the most valuable aspects of this e-book is the real-world use cases it presents. Find out how different lines of business can leverage SAP Business Data Cloud to unleash transformative insights, connect data, and foster reliable AI, including:  

  • Core enterprise analytics: Monitor financial health, refine cash flow, and improve payment terms for agile decision-making. 
  • People analytics: Evaluate workforce trends, track talent flows, and align HR and business strategies to boost engagement and productivity. 
  • Spend analytics: Gain visibility into spending, mitigate risks, and optimize procurement for maximum value. 

Read the E-Book 

Discover how an integrated approach to data management delivers the context and insights to help businesses make actionable decisions quickly. Download the Unleash Transformative Insights with SAP Business Data Cloud today and learn more about harnessing the power of semantically rich data.
